name: Adjustable Lens Refractor Gun
descriptionHtml:
An artifice gun with cartridges that lets it fire blasts of arcane energy through a series of lenses rather than bullets. Attacks with this weapon deal Arcane damage. 

By spending 1 AP per lens, the user may adjust the strength of the gun at the cost of accuracy. Each lens adjusted for power lets the user treat their attacks with the weapon as if their power was 1 higher than it actually is, at the cost of their focus being treated as 1 lower than it actually is. Up to 3 lenses can be modified in this way. 

Exists in Musket and Sniper Rifle variants.

Mods:

Also takes normal musket and sniper rifle mods.

  • A mods:
    • Elemental Lens (10 points): Instead of Arcane, the weapon may deal a different element of your choosing
  • B mods:
    • None

name: Advanced Surgery Kit
descriptionHtml:
Surgery kits contain stitches, bandages and other items used for more serious wounds.

Rest Action: You perform minor surgery on an ally or yourself. Requires at least 5 points in Medicine (or a relevant expertise in Medicine) to use. Make a medicine check against DC 15. On success you can negate the effect of one injury suffered this campaign until the end of the campaign. An injury can only be negated once by this effect, and one person can only have one negated injury at a time.  The injury must still be dealt with after the campaign ends.

With an advanced surgery kit, you can heal two injuries from a single person instead of just one. The DC to heal two injuries is DC30.

name: Adventurer's Kit
descriptionHtml:
Adventuring kits contain all of the basic tools for adventuring. Included in the kit: Backpack, Flint and steel, Rope, Notebook, Pen, Compass, Bedroll, Torches, Hand Mirror.

Effect: You can use the items for their various purposes within reason. Can store up to 5 burden of Adventuring Essentials, a Northi Lantern or items included in this kit in a combat ready state for 0 burden and contains a torch/lantern holster for hands off torch/lantern use for light purposes. Cannot store kits or other burden reducing items in this combat ready state.

name: Aerial Staff (Custom Model)
descriptionHtml:
A custom model aerial staff has no fixed cost as it is made up from several parts. There are several base models that can be further modified through the use of parts.

Adding, removing or changing the parts of an aerial staff requires a level 8 artificer to spend an EP in town, though they may add and/or remove as many parts from a single staff as they want with that one EP.

For information on staves and how they work, check the rulebook.

All parts and staff models can be crafted by artificers and magitechnicians as if they were normal artifices. Unless stated otherwise, parts cannot be modified.

Unless stated otherwise, all aerial staff models have 3 burden, though this can be reduced with C mods like normal.

Models:


Falcon Model
Cost: 5000c
Slots:

  • Main Weapon S
  • Sub Weapon S
  • Sub Weapon S
  • Utility S
  • Utility S
  • Engine S


Egret Model
Cost: 5000c
Slots:

  • Main Weapon S
  • Sub Weapon S
  • Utility S
  • Utility S
  • Utility S
  • Engine S


Kestrel Model
Cost: 3000c
Slots:

  • Main Weapon S
  • Main Weapon S
  • Sub Weapon S
  • Engine S
Main Weapons

Sorthern Arc Buster Cannon
Ammo Capacity: 2. 1 Shell per use
Fixed Weapon, 0 RP, 60ft range, Strafe 50
You fire a blast of energy, making a heavy attack that deals arcane damage. This attack has the shieldbreaker property.
Cost: 1000c

Napalm Bomb
Ammo Capacity: 2. 1 Shell per use
Fixed Weapon, 0 RP, 90ft range, Strafe 25
You hurl a fireball at a 15x15ft area, making a Light attack that deals fire damage against all units in the area. On damage, you inflict Burning 5.
Cost: 750c

Sub Weapons

Magic Rocket Pods
Ammo Capacity: 2. 1 Shell per use
Turret Weapon, 0 RP, 30ft range
You fire a highly accurate blast of arcane energy at the target, making a Precise Attack that deals Arcane damage against the target. This attack has the Lock On property, but does not get pinpoint
Cost: 500c

Blaze Rifle
Gimballed Weapon, 120ft range, Strafe 10
Rapid, you make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target.
Cost: 1000c

Northi Gattling Rifle
Fixed Weapon, 30ft range, Strafe 50
1 AP, Make a light attack against the target and all targets 10ft directly behind it.
Cost: 500c

Sorthen Smoke Pod
Ammo Capacity: 1. 1 Shell per use
Turret Weapon, 0 RP, 30ft range
You create a sphere of smoke in a 15x15ft area that blocks line of sight. This effect functions similarly to Glittershards and lasts until the start of your next turn.
Cost: 1250c
 

Utility

Sorthen Shield Projector
Your staff gains a 25 HP shield. While the shield lasts, your staff does not lose altitude from any attacks that are made against the shield.
Cost: 1250c

Sorthen Ejection System
Ammo Capacity: 1. 1 Shell per use
0 RP, You immediately trigger wind bubble for both yourself and your artifice
Cost: 1000c

Medivac Rig
You can haul a downed ally on the underbelly of your staff, but your speed is halved while doing so.
Cost: 500c

Afterburner
Ammo Capacity: 1. 1 Shell per use
You double the speed of your staff for the next full movement action.
Cost: 1250c

Engine

Budget Model Engine
20ft air speed, no line movement. 1 Medium Shell per day.
Cost: 250c

Stock Model Engine
65ft air speed, 10ft turn. 1 Medium Shell per day
Cost: 750c

Arlan Custom Engine
35ft air speed, no line movement. 1 Medium Shell per day
Cost: 1500c

Wing Model Engine
50ft air speed, 30ft turn. 1 Medium Shell per day
Cost: 1000c

name: Aerial Staff (Sparrow Model)
descriptionHtml:
The Sparrow Model Aerial Staff design is the most simple entry level Aerial Staff.  It has a base speed of 20 feet and does not include integrated shielding or any weapon systems.  Often used for training, its fuel efficiency is fairly high. 

Its user can use it to fly. It has a speed of a 20ft.

Requires 1 hand to operate while flying.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Jury-Rigged Cannon (10 points): Adds a small star cannon that fires an arcane blast from the front that deals heavy attack damage for 2 AP with 120ft Range. 
    • Medivac Rig (20 points): Allows a downed ally to be hauled on the underbelly of the staff. But reduces the speed by 10ft while hauling. 
    • Jury Rigged Shield (35 points): Adds a shield generator, granting the airship a 15 HP shield. This shield can be switched on and off, but can only be replenished by changing the fuel cell. 
  • B mods:
    • Sorthen Shield Generator (50 points): Requires Jury-Rigged Shield. Installs a Sorthen style Shield Generator increasing the shield value by 15 HP.
    • Upgraded Airship Engine (80 points): Increases the speed to 50ft but adds Line Movement: The airship may only move in a straight line when making a movement action.

name: Aetherie Sensory Station
descriptionHtml: A large stationary artifice chair an Aetherie connects themselves to that drastically boosts Mind Connection range by 250 miles.
descriptionText: A large stationary artifice chair an Aetherie connects themselves to that drastically boosts Mind Connection range by 250 miles.

name: Alchemist's Alembic
descriptionHtml: Core crafting tool. Allows you to craft items as an alchemist.
descriptionText: Core crafting tool. Allows you to craft items as an alchemist.

name: Alchemy Materials
descriptionHtml:
Alchemy Herbs:

Alchemy Herb Mixing

When crafting as an alchemist, you may add additional herbs and alchemy materials into the mix. By adding in an extra amount of alchemy units as well as the exact same herbs as the recipe needs, you get extra dice.

  • Small Herb Mix
    • Required extra's: 2x recipe's original cost.
    • Effect: +1 Crafting Dice
  • Medium Herb Mix
    • Required extra's: 4x recipe's original cost.
    • Effect: +2 Crafting Dice
  • Large Herb Mix
    • Required extra's: 8x recipe's original cost.
    • Effect: +3 Crafting Dice
  • Supreme Herb Mix
    • Required extra's: 14x recipe's original cost.
    • Effect: +4 Crafting Dice
descriptionText:

name: Alchemy Rig
descriptionHtml:
A carrier that allows easy access to Elixirs, Flasks, Potions, Poisons and Salves. You can only equip one alchemy rig at a time, whether it's the normal alchemy rig or the deluxe version.

Stores up to 2 burden worth of Elixirs, Flasks, Potions and Salves.
descriptionText:
A carrier that allows easy access to Elixirs, Flasks, Potions, Poisons and Salves. You can only equip one alchemy rig at a time, whether it's the normal alchemy rig or the deluxe version.

Stores up to 2 burden worth of Elixirs, Flasks, Potions and Salves.

name: Alchemy Rig (Deluxe)
descriptionHtml:
A carrier that allows easy access to Elixirs, Flasks, Potions, Poisons and Salves. You can only equip one alchemy rig at a time, whether it's the normal alchemy rig or the deluxe version.

Stores up to 5 burden worth of Elixirs, Flasks, Potions and Salves.

name: Anti-Air Weapon Sight
descriptionHtml:
An artifice designed to combat against aerial mages. Provides tracking assistance against the more predictable movement of air units. 

Attaches to ranged weapons. 

Increases the accuracy of ranged weapon attacks against flying targets by +4 until the start of your next turn.

Can be attached or detached by spending 4 AP.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Holographic Optic (10 points): Increases the accuracy against flying targets by +1
  • B mods:
    • Wide Targeting (25 points): The accuracy bonus is reduced by 2, but it now works on all targets.
    • Aerial Targeting Mode (50 points): The effect now lasts until the end of the encounter or until deactivated by spending 2 AP. However, attacks against non-flying targets suffer a -4 penalty.
    • Wing Outlining (80 points): The first attack made after activation applies the downburst effect (See: Downburst ability)

name: Antidote Shot Potion
descriptionHtml:
Created with Goblin Medical Science, a universal antidote for all common poisons.  Stored inside of a glass vial syringe that is injected into the body. When used, immediately removes a single common poison effect and grants immunity to that poison for the next hour.

Crafting:
Materials: 1400 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Earth
  • 1x Rare Water

name: Armor (Clothing)
descriptionHtml:
A high quality set of clothing. Does not provide any bonuses by itself, but if crafted by an armorsmith, can accept Flamellon mods.

Cannot be worn at the same time as regular armor.

Mods:

  • A mods
    • Mage Robes (20 points): You gain +1 maximum mana, but lose 1 guard and 2 block.
  • B mods
    • Cloud Robes (50 points): Your movement speed is increased by 5ft.
    • Battle Casting (80 points): The first spell you cast each turn does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
  • Frame mods
    • Reinforced Clothing (0 points): This clothing can benefit from material bonuses, but its burden is increased by 1.

name: Armor (Heavy)
descriptionHtml:
Increases your Guard by 3 and your Block by 12, but reduces your Initiative by 3 and your Evasion and Dodge by 6.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Beacon Armor (35 points): Unarmored allies (except armor plates) within 40ft of you gain +1 guard.
  • B mods:
    • Deflecting Armor (25 points): An accuracy roll against you that rolls a 1 always misses.
    • Weighted Greaves (50 points): You become immovable while on the ground.
    • Titan Armor (50 points): You are treated as having a size category 1 step higher (maximum Large).
  • Frame mods:
    • Berserker Armor II (10 points): This armor reduces your RP by 3, but does not reduce your evasion and dodge.
    • Fortress (20 points): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but it also reduces the movement speed by 5ft.

name: Armor (Light)
descriptionHtml:
Increases your Guard by 1 and your Block by 4, but reduces your Initiative by 1 and your Evasion and Dodge by 2.

Mods:

  • A mods
    • Agile Weave (35 points): No armor penalty, but no guard/block bonus. You gain +1 evasion (but no additional dodge). This also takes up your Frame mod slot.
    • Acrobat's Harness (10 points): You gain +10 bonus to athletics while wearing this armor.
    • Camo (10 points): Pick a color theme. You gain +5 to stealth when hiding in environments of that theme, but -5 when hiding elsewhere. A GM may approve new environments, defaults are: green camo (jungle, woodland, prone in grass), yellow camo (desert, savannas), white camo (snow, arctic), dark camo (night, caves, rocky terrain), blue camo (rivers or oceanic)
    • Porcupine Hide (10 points): Enemies grappling you take 1d6 full pierce damage. When escaping a grapple, you also deal 1d6 full pierce damage to the creature grappling you.
  • B mods
    • Sleek (25 points): If you spend RP dodging an attack of opportunity, you may dodge any other attacks of opportunity made in reaction to the same action at the first for free.
    • Padded (25 points): Increase guard vs light attacks by 1, doesn't affect heavy damage.
    • Mistweave (50 points): You no longer provoke attacks of opportunity, but all attacks against you gain Half Pierce.

name: Armor (Medium)
descriptionHtml:
Increases your Guard by 2 and your Block by 8, but reduces your Initiative by 2 and your Evasion and Dodge by 4.

Mods:

  • A mods:
    • Weak Point Protection (20 Points): Pinpoint attacks against you reduce 1 less guard.
  • B mods:
    • Chain Links (25 points): Increases the block gained from this armor by 4.
    • Flexible Armor (50 points): When you take the Dodge action, your next Block gains a +8 bonus. When you take the Block action, your next Dodge action gains a +4 bonus. Lasts until the start of your next turn. Can activate multiple times per turn, but does not stack.
  • Frame mods:
    • Berserker Armor (10 points): This armor reduces your RP by 2, but does not reduce your evasion and dodge.

name: Armor Plates
descriptionHtml:
Can be attached to an armor to increase its guard, but make it bulkier.

Gives the armor they are attached to +1 Guard (and +2 block), but increases the Burden of it by 3.

Attaching it or removing it from an armor takes 10 minutes.

Can be used together with other armor modifications. Can be put on regular clothing, but always requires an armor proficiency to have an effect. While you can wear multiple armor plates, the effect does not stack.

Despite being craftable by an armorsmith, the item cannot be modified during crafting.

name: Armorsmithing Materials
descriptionHtml:
Armorsmithing Base Metal Materials

The base material for crafting Medium and Heavy armor with armorsmithing is Iron. Iron units must be converted into an ingot before they can be used.

  • Iron:
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 300 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 500 Iron units
    • Effect: None
    • When using Iron to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot for shield or armor plates, 2 ingots for a medium armor or greatshield or 4 for a heavy armor.

Outside of iron, there are a variety of special materials that an armorsmith can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant stat bonuses.

  • Tamahagane
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 1050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Tamahagane units.
    • Effect: +1 stat bonus
    • When using Tamahagane to craft an item, you must spend 1 of Tamahagane. If crafting a medium armor or great shield, you must add 1 ingot of Iron. If crafting a heavy armor, you must add 3 ingots of Iron.
  • Mithril
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 4050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 8000 Mithril units.
    • Effect: +1/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Mithril to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Mithril. If crafting a medium armor or great shield, you must add 1 ingot of Iron. If crafting a heavy armor, you must add 3 ingots of Iron.
  • Orichalcum
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 8050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 16000 Orihalcum units.
    • Effect: +2/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Orihalcum to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Orihalcum. If crafting a medium armor or great shield, you must add 1 ingot of Iron. If crafting a heavy armor, you must add 3 ingots of Iron.
  • Escudo
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 11550 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 23000 Escudo units.
    • Effect: +2/+2 stat bonus
    • When using Escudo to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Escudo. If crafting a medium armor or great shield, you must add 1 ingot of Iron. If crafting a heavy armor, you must add 3 ingots of Iron.

Stat bonuses:

Armor: Each stat bonus gives a +1 evasion and dodge Material bonus or a +1 guard and +2 block Material bonus. A mithril or primehide armor would thus give +1 evasion, dodge and guard as well as +2 Block.
Shield: Each stat bonus gives a +1 Gear bonus to Dodge or a +3 Gear bonus to Block. 

Armorsmithing Base Leather Materials

The base material for crafting clothing, light armor and cloaks is Animal Hides. Animal Hides must be converted into Tanned Animal Hide before they can be used.

IMPORTANT: Clothing does not gain material bonuses when made out of something better unless they have the Reinforced Clothing A mod.

  • Animal Hide:
    • Clim Cost per tanned hide: 300 Clim
    • Unit cost per tanned hide: 500 Animal Hide units
    • Effect: None
    • When using animal hides to craft an item, you must spend 1 tanned animal hide for clothing, light armor, bags and cloaks.

Outside of animal hides, there are a variety of special materials that an armorsmith can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant stat bonuses.

  • Direhide:
    • Clim Cost per tanned hide: 1050 Clim
    • Unit cost per tanned hide: 2000 Direhide units
    • Effect: +1 stat bonus
    • When using direhides to craft an item, you must spend 1 tanned direhide for clothing, light armor and cloaks.
  • Primehide:
    • Clim Cost per tanned hide: 4050 Clim
    • Unit cost per tanned hide: 8000 Primehide units
    • Effect: +1/+1 stat bonus
    • When using primehides to craft an item, you must spend 1 tanned primehides for clothing, light armor and cloaks.
  • Dragonscale:
    • Clim Cost per tanned hide: 8050 Clim
    • Unit cost per tanned hide: 16000 Dragonscale units
    • Effect: +2/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Dragonscales to craft an item, you must spend 1 tanned Dragonscale for clothing, light armor and cloaks.
  • Astrahide:
    • Clim Cost per tanned hide: 11550 Clim
    • Unit cost per tanned hide: 23000 Astrahide units
    • Effect: +2/+2 stat bonus
    • When using Astrahide to craft an item, you must spend 1 tanned Astrahide for clothing, light armor and cloaks.

Stat bonuses:

Armor: Each stat bonus gives a +1 evasion and dodge Material bonus or a +1 guard and +2 block Material bonus. A mithril or primehide armor would thus give +1 evasion, dodge and guard as well as +2 Block.

Armorsmithing Special Materials

  • Spidersilk (500 Clim / 15 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost: 1000 Spidersilk units
    • A Spidersilk armor reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but the wearer takes double damage from Fire damage. Light armor only.
  • Electrum (3050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 6000 Electrum units.
    • Armors infused with Electrum gain  a +1 Guard and +4 block bonus against attacks made by Divines, Summons and Otherworlders.
  • Silver (1050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Silver units.
    • Silver infused armors remove a Fiend's regeneration. When a fiend hits you with a melee attack or a physical contact attack, they lose their regeneration until the start of their next turn.
  • Mycelite (1550 Clim / 20 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Mycelite units.
    • Mycelite infused armor incur 1 less burden to a minimum of 1.
  • Adamantite (2050 Clim / 20 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 4000 Adamantite units
    • Adamantite armors cannot be destroyed or damaged by effect such as Sunder by non adamantite tools or weapons.
  • Luxaeterna (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Luxaeterna units.
    • Armor infused with Luxaterna gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Holy damage.
  • Corvuskite (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Corvuskite units. 
    • Armor infused with Corvuskite gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Dark damage.
  • Arcanium (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Arcanium units. 
    • Armor infused with Arcanium gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Arcane damage.
  • Vivinthal (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Vivinthal units. 
    • Armor infused with Vivinthal gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Water damage.
  • Glacite (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Glacite units. 
    • Armor infused with Glacite gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Frost damage.
  • Salachar (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Salachar units. 
    • Armor infused with Salachar gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Fire damage.
  • Sylphsteel (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Sylphsteel units. 
    • Armor infused with Sylphsteel gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Wind damage.
  • Fulgarite ( 1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Fulgarite units. 
    • Armor infused with Fulgarite gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Lightning damage.
  • Ghimslag (1550 Clim / 30 Crafting Points): 
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Ghimslag units. 
    • Armor infused with Ghimslag gives the wearer 5 damage reduction against Earth damage.
  • Crystal Core (3500 Clim / 35 Crafting Points)
    • Unit cost: 1 core
    • Armor infused with the core of a specific fiend. The wearer may spend 1 Mana and 0 RP to absorb the energy of an elemental (Arcane, Fire, Water, Wind, Earth or any of its subtypes) damage attack. Their next weapon attack gains bonus damage equal to the attackers power and becomes the element of the triggering attack.
  • Nullite ( 2050 Clim / 20 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 4000 Nullite units
    • Anti-Magic Layer: Against spells, you gain 2 guard, 2 evasion and a +2 bonus to saves. However you can no longer become the target of friendly spells (including your own).
  • Runesteel ( 1050 Clim / 10 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Runesteel units
    • Select Magic Absorption: Friendly spells targeting you gain +20ft range.
  • Sunsteel ( 1550 Clim, 15 crafting points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Sunsteel units
    • Sun Protection: You are immune to any negative effects suffered from being in the sun.
  • Perfect Shell (2000 Clim)
    • Can be made into light armor or armor plates by a Master Armorer (counts as Mithril in terms of difficulty).
    • If used for light armor, the light armor cannot have another special material (may still have flamelon mods) and cannot have frame mods, but it receives +1 guard and +4 Block and may have one medium armor property. Counts as a base material for crafting as well.
    • If used for armor plates, the armor plates receive an additional 2 block for a +1 guard and +4 block total.

Armorsmithing Cloak Materials

Cloaks may benefit from higher base materials being used. This is on a per cloak basis and is listed with the material.

  • No special material
    • A cloak made with no special material may gain a Gear bonus to its Dodge or Block.
    • When made with Direhide/Primehide/Dragonscale/Astrahide gives a +1/+2/+3/+4 Gear bonus to Dodge or a +3/+6/+9/+12 Gear bonus to Block.
  • Night Wolf Pelt (550 Clim / 5 Crafting Points)
    • Unit cost: 1000 units
    • A cloak of night wolf pelt gives the wielder +5 bonus to stealth checks during the night or in area's without light.
    • When made with Direhide/Primehide instead gives a +10/+15 bonus.
  • Fae Wolf Pelt (1050 Clim / 10 Crafting Points)
    • Unit Cost: 2000 Units
    • A cloak of faewolf pelt gives the wielder the ability to spend 4 AP to hide in plain sight. This hide lasts until you take any other action or speak.
    • When made with Primehide, reduce the AP cost to 3.
  • Astral Tiger Pelt (1050 Clim / 10 Crafting Points)
    • Unit Cost: 2000 Units
    • A cloak of astral tiger pelt gives the wielder the ability to teleport when attacked. After an attack against you misses (or you dodge it), you may spend 1 RP to teleport up to 20ft.
  • Wonderland Rabbit Hide (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting Points) (By CCeles)
    • Unit Cost: No units, just 1 hide.
    • When you take a movement action that costs RP, that movement is increased by 5ft. (Doesn't stack with Compact Ergonomics mod)
    • When made with Primehide, the movement is increased by 10ft instead.

name: Artifice Limb
descriptionHtml:
An artifice item that replaces a lost limb or body part. Each of these must be custom made for a person. When attached, it allows the user to function as if the artifice limb was a normal limb.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Grapple Launcher (Arm only)(10 points): Allows the user to install a grappling hook artifice in the arm, functioning just like the grappling hook would. (Grappling hook must be supplied separately and takes an errand action from an artificer to install). A grappling hook installed in the arm takes burden like usual and still takes an AP to activate, but requires no AP to pull out.
    • Zoom In (Eye only)(20 points): You may spend 1 AP to gain a +10 bonus to your perception until the start of your next turn.
    • Weapon Slot (Arm only)(20 points): Allows the user to install a weapon into the arm, functioning just like the weapon would (takes an errand from artificer to install). The weapon costs burden like normal, but may be drawn or sheathed for 0 AP once per turn.
    • Movement Burst (Leg only) (20 points): You may spend 0 AP to gain +20ft movement until the start of your next turn. Afterwards, your movement speed is halved until you put in a new shell into your leg.
  • B mods:
    • Blast Knuckle (Arm or Leg only) (25 points): After making a Heavy unarmed attack with the limb, you may spend 0 RP to make a Light attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide, 15ft long cone behind the target. After doing this, you must put in a new shell into the limb to use it again (your movement speed is halved if your leg is unusable).
    • Rocket Punch (Arm only) (50 points): Once per round, you may make a basic unarmed Heavy attack with this limb to a target within 60ft.
    • Thermal Fusion Sight (Eye only)(80 points): Adds thermal vision up to 120ft. This cannot detect things hidden by magical means.  The sight can not discern a difference in heat once something reaches freezing temperatures.

name: Artifice Water Purifier
descriptionHtml:
A filter that purifies water to make it safe to drink. The device is easy to use and can filter up to 2 liters of water in 1 minute.  

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Filter (10 points): Can filter up to 4 liters of water in 1 minute.
  • B mods:
    • Natural Use (25 points): When used during a rest action, the filter provides half the food needed for a basic rest (20u) provided a camping kit is set up.

name: Artificer Materials
descriptionHtml:
The base material for crafting items with Artificing is Iron Artifice parts.  You can either buy Iron Artifice parts or convert them from Iron by using the Artificer or Transmuter abilities.

Artifice weapons follow special rules. Weapon attachments follow the regular artifice rules however.

Iron:
Equal artifice parts to the base clim cost of the artifice is required to craft Iron Artifices.

Outside of iron, there are a variety of special materials that an Artificer can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant additional C mod slots to the artifice.

You need Artifice Parts of the matching type to craft and the amount you need is determined this way:

(Special Material Modifier) x (Base Clim Cost) = Amount of Special Material Artifice Parts Needed

  • Iron Artifice Parts
    • Multiplier: 1x
    • Effect: 1 C mod slot
  • Tamahagane Artifice Parts
    • Multiplier: 1x
    • Effect: 2 C mod slots
  • Mithril Artifice Parts
    • Multiplier: 2x
    • Effect: 3 C mod slots
  • Orichalcum Artifice Parts
    • Multiplier: 2x
    • Effect: 4 C mod slots
  • Escudo Artifice Parts
    • Multiplier 3x
    • Effect: 5 C mod slots

Artificing Weapon Materials:

The base material for crafting weapons such as pistols or sniper rifles with artificing is Iron. Iron units must be converted into an ingot before they can be used.

  • Iron:
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 300 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 500 Iron units
    • Effect: None
    • When using Iron to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot.

Outside of iron, there are a variety of special materials that an artificer can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant stat bonuses.

NOTE: When making an Artifice weapon that is an actual weapon and not an attachment, you must spend an additional iron artifice parts equal to the cost of the weapon - 500 rather than an extra ingot. This also applies to guns.

  • Tamahagane
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 1050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Tamahagane units.
    • Effect: +1 stat bonus
    • When using Tamahagane to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Tamahagane.
  • Mithril
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 4050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 8000 Mithril units.
    • Effect: +1/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Mithril to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Mithril.
  • Orichalcum
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 8050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 16000 Orihalcum units.
    • Effect: +2/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Orihalcum to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Orihalcum.
  • Escudo
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 11550 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 23000 Escudo units.
    • Effect: +2/+2 stat bonus
    • When using Escudo to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Escudo.

Stat bonuses depend on if the crafted item is a tool or weapon.

Weapon: The Material bonuses to go Focus and Power. With getting a +1 bonus or a +2/+1 bonus, the crafter may choose which of the two stats gets the extra bonus.

Artificing special materials

  • Dark Iron (550 Clim / 15 Crafting Points) :
    • Unit cost per ingot:  1000 Dark Iron units.
    • Strikes with Dark Iron infused weapons cause the target to be unable to teleport until the end of their next turn.
  • Silver (1050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Silver units.
    • Dealing damage with Silver infused weapons disables the Regeneration effect of Fiends until the start of your next turn.
  • Electrum (3050 Clim/ 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 6000 Electrum units.
    • Strikes with Electrum infused weapons grant a +2 Electrum Power bonus against Divines, Summons or Otherworlders.
  • Mycelite (1550 Clim / 20 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Mycelite units.
    • Mycelite infused weapons incur 1 less burden to a minimum of 1
  • Obsidian Steel (550 Clim)
    • Unit cost per ingot:  1000 Obsidian Steel units.
    • Obsidian Steel infused weapons cannot be the targets of magical effects.
  • Adamantite (2050 Clim / 20 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per ingot: 4000 Adamantite units
    • Adamantite weapons cannot be destroyed by non adamantite tools or weapons.
  • Luxaeterna (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Luxaeterna units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Holy.
  • Corvuskite (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Corvuskite units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Dark.
  • Arcanium (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Arcanium units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Arcane.
  • Fulgarite ( 1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points )
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Fulgarite units
    • Changes the physical damage type into Lightning.
  • Vivinthal (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Vivinthal units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Water.
  • Glacite (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)

    • Changes the physical damage type into Frost.
  • Salachar (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)

    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Ghimslag units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Earth.
  • Nullite ( 2050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 4000 Nullite units
    • All attacks made with this weapon gain the Shieldbreaker property.
  • Runesteel ( 1050 Clim / 10 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Runesteel units
    • This weapon can channel spells as if it was a channeling weapon.
  • Sepsium ( 2050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 4000 Sepsium units
    • When damaging a creature with this weapon, they cannot recover HP (except for regeneration type effects) until the start of your next turn.
  • Sunsteel ( 1550 Clim / 15 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Sunsteel units
    • Attacks with this weapon gain Full Pierce against creatures weak to light or sunlight.
descriptionText:
The base material for crafting items with Artificing is Iron Artifice parts.  You can either buy Iron Artifice parts or convert them from Iron by using the Artificer or Transmuter abilities.

Artifice weapons follow special rules. Weapon attachments follow the regular artifice rules however.

Iron:
Equal artifice parts to the base clim cost of the artifice is required to craft Iron Artifices.

Outside of iron, there are a variety of special materials that an Artificer can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant additional C mod slots to the artifice.

You need Artifice Parts of the matching type to craft and the amount you need is determined this way:

(Special Material Modifier) x (Base Clim Cost) = Amount of Special Material Artifice Parts Needed

- Iron Artifice Parts- Multiplier: 1x
- Effect: 1 C mod slot

- Tamahagane Artifice Parts- Multiplier: 1x
- Effect: 2 C mod slots

- Mithril Artifice Parts- Multiplier: 2x
- Effect: 3 C mod slots

- Orichalcum Artifice Parts- Multiplier: 2x
- Effect: 4 C mod slots

- Escudo Artifice Parts- Multiplier 3x
- Effect: 5 C mod slots

Artificing Weapon Materials:

The base material for crafting weapons such as pistols or sniper rifles with artificing is Iron. Iron units must be converted into an ingot before they can be used.

- Iron:- Clim Cost per ingot: 300 Clim
- Unit cost per ingot: 500 Iron units
- Effect: None
- When using Iron to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot.

Outside of iron, there are a variety of special materials that an artificer can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant stat bonuses.

NOTE: When making an Artifice weapon that is an actual weapon and not an attachment, you must spend an additional iron artifice parts equal to the cost of the weapon - 500 rather than an extra ingot. This also applies to guns.

- Tamahagane- Clim Cost per ingot: 1050 Clim
- Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Tamahagane units.
- Effect: +1 stat bonus
- When using Tamahagane to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Tamahagane.

- Mithril- Clim Cost per ingot: 4050 Clim
- Unit cost per ingot: 8000 Mithril units.
- Effect: +1/+1 stat bonus
- When using Mithril to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Mithril.

- Orichalcum- Clim Cost per ingot: 8050 Clim
- Unit cost per ingot: 16000 Orihalcum units.
- Effect: +2/+1 stat bonus
- When using Orihalcum to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Orihalcum.

- Escudo- Clim Cost per ingot: 11550 Clim
- Unit cost per ingot: 23000 Escudo units.
- Effect: +2/+2 stat bonus
- When using Escudo to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Escudo.

Stat bonuses depend on if the crafted item is a tool or weapon.

Weapon: The Material bonuses to go Focus and Power. With getting a +1 bonus or a +2/+1 bonus, the crafter may choose which of the two stats gets the extra bonus.

Artificing special materials

- Dark Iron (550 Clim / 15 Crafting Points) :- Unit cost per ingot:  1000 Dark Iron units.
- Strikes with Dark Iron infused weapons cause the target to be unable to teleport until the end of their next turn.

- Silver (1050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):- Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Silver units.
- Dealing damage with Silver infused weapons disables the Regeneration effect of Fiends until the start of your next turn.

- Electrum (3050 Clim/ 30 Crafting Points):- Unit cost per ingot: 6000 Electrum units.
- Strikes with Electrum infused weapons grant a +2 Electrum Power bonus against Divines, Summons or Otherworlders.

- Mycelite (1550 Clim / 20 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Mycelite units.
- Mycelite infused weapons incur 1 less burden to a minimum of 1

- Obsidian Steel (550 Clim)- Unit cost per ingot:  1000 Obsidian Steel units.
- Obsidian Steel infused weapons cannot be the targets of magical effects.

- Adamantite (2050 Clim / 20 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 4000 Adamantite units
- Adamantite weapons cannot be destroyed by non adamantite tools or weapons.

- Luxaeterna (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Luxaeterna units.
- Changes the physical damage type into Holy.

- Corvuskite (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Corvuskite units.
- Changes the physical damage type into Dark.

- Arcanium (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Arcanium units.
- Changes the physical damage type into Arcane.

- Fulgarite ( 1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points )- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Fulgarite units
- Changes the physical damage type into Lightning.

- Vivinthal (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Vivinthal units.
- Changes the physical damage type into Water.

- Glacite (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Glacite units.
- Changes the physical damage type into Frost.

- Salachar (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Salachar units.
- Changes the physical damage type into Fire.

- Sylphsteel (1550 Clim / 15 Crafting points)- Unit cost per ingot: 3000 Sylphsteel units
- Changes the physical damage type into Wind.

name: Artificer's Kit
descriptionHtml:
Artificer’s Patch kit with various tools for maintaining artifices.

Rest Action: You can either patch a broken, non-airship Artifice (with the exception of Aerial Staves), allowing it to be usable until the end of the scenario if its repairable. The Artifice still counts as broken at the end of the scenario and cannot be patched again until fully repaired.  Alternatively, you can instead choose to create an Artifice Shell, paying its cost in Magical Fuel to create.

name: Artificer's Multitool
descriptionHtml: Core crafting tool. Allows you to craft items as an artificer.
descriptionText: Core crafting tool. Allows you to craft items as an artificer.

name: Astra Guidance Beacon
descriptionHtml:
While this artifice is in effect, the spirit core values of your allies within 300ft of you are treated as 500 higher than they actually are for the purposes of calculating astra corruption risks. Does not stack with similar resistance boosting effects and only works on allies with a Spirit Core lower than yours.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Increased Effect (20 points): Now boosts the spirit core values of your allies by 1000 instead of 500.
  • B mods:
    • Exousiai Protection (80 points): You and allies affected by this device ignore 1 level of astra corruption increase caused by Exousiai type fiends.

name: Axe (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Wild Swing: When attacking a target, you still deal 4 (Light Attack) or 8 (Heavy/Precise Attack) damage if the attack misses. This is reduced by guard (to a minimum of 1). On damage effects do not trigger from this damage.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Reinforced Langet (10 points): This weapon cannot be disarmed.
    • Bearded Extension (20 points): Wild Swing now ignores guard, but the damage is reduced to 2 (light attack) or 4 (heavy attack).
    • Rotational Stability (35 points): If a target moves away from you when you attack with this weapon and your next attack this turn is a throwing attack using this weapon against that target, that attack gains lock on.
  • B mods:
    • Boomerang Wing (25 points): After using a throwing attack with this weapon, you can return the weapon to your hand for 0 RP.
    • Lancebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Lancebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding polearms or lances.

name: Axolotl Elixir
descriptionHtml:
When drunk, you take true damage equal to your Toughness x 5. Until the end of the encounter, you regain HP equal to your Toughness at the start of your turn.

If the triggering damage lowers your HP to 0 or lower, the effect immediately ends. Your HP being dropped to 0 or lower from other effects does not cancel this elixir.

Crafting:
Materials: 870 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Water
  • 1x Common Arcane

descriptionHtml:
Mounted on your back or a pack animal. 

Allows you to store items in non-combat storage.

name: Bear Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that raises your stamina and reaction speed. When drunk, you take 11 true damage. You immediately gain 1 RP and your maximum RP increases by 1 until the end of the encounter.

Crafting:
Materials: 650 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Earth

name: Binoculars
descriptionHtml:
Binoculars let you zoom in on distant views, bringing faraway details up close and clear. 

When using these, you gain a +10 bonus on perception checks against things further than 60ft away but a -10 penalty to things within 60ft.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Focus Dial (20 points): The penalty for things within 60ft is reduced from -10 to -5.
    • Night Mode (20 points): Looking through the binoculars gives you Nightvision up to 120ft.
  • B mods:
    • Range Finder (25 points): When looking through this item, you can tell how far objects are from you.
    • Anti-Illusion Lens (50 points): You gain a +10 bonus to determine if something is an Illusion or not.

name: Blacksmithing Materials
descriptionHtml:
Blacksmithing Base Materials

The base material for crafting items with blacksmithing is Iron. Iron units must be converted into an ingot before they can be used.

  • Iron:
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 300 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 500 Iron units
    • Effect: None
    • When using Iron to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot for a one handed weapon or tool, or 2 for a longsword or two handed weapon (or two handed tool).

Outside of iron, there are a variety of special materials that a blacksmith can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant stat bonuses

  • Tamahagane
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 1050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 2000 Tamahagane units.
    • Effect: +1 stat bonus
    • When using Tamahagane to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Tamahagane. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional ingot of Iron.
  • Mithril
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 4050 Clim
    • Unit cost per ingot: 8000 Mithril units.
    • Effect: +1/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Mithril to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Mithril. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional ingot of Iron.
  • Orichalcum
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 8050 Clim

  • Escudo
    • Clim Cost per ingot: 11550 Clim

    • Effect: +2/+2 stat bonus
    • When using Escudo to craft an item, you must spend 1 ingot of Escudo. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional ingot of Iron.

Stat bonuses depend on if the crafted item is a tool or weapon.

Weapon: The Material bonuses to go Focus and Power. With getting a +1 bonus or a +2/+1 bonus, the crafter may choose which of the two stats gets the extra bonus.

Tool: For tools, there is a crafting bonus and a finish bonus. A crafting bonus gives you +1 (or +2) Material bonus to each crafting dice roll. The finish bonus gives you a +5 (or +10) Finish bonus once at the end of your craft.

Blacksmithing Special Materials

When these materials are added to a crafting tool, they give a +1 bonus to crafting checks when working with that material.

  • Dark Iron (550 Clim / 15 Crafting Points) :

name: Blaster Weapon
descriptionHtml:
An artifice that fires a condensed blast of magical energy. Used to give a ranged option to melee weapons. Attachable to melee weapons. 

When activated, changes the weapon to a ranged mode that can fire elemental blasts to a target up to 80ft away like a ranged weapon could. The elemental type is determined at artifice creation. When the weapon is activated again, the weapon switches back to the melee mode and the shell is expended.

Can be attached or detached from a melee weapon by spending 4 AP.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Swap (20 points): The activation cost is reduced to 0 AP.
  • B mods:
    • Lightweight (25 points): Reduces the Burden of this artifice by 1.
    • Sniper Mode (50 points): The range of the ranged mode is increased to 240ft.
    • Channeling Blasts (80 points): Your ranged mode also counts as a channeling weapon for the purpose of casting spells through it, as if the weapon it is attached to was a channeling weapon.
descriptionText:

When the weapon is activated again, the weapon switches back to the melee mode and the shell is expended.

Can be attached or detached from a melee weapon by spending 4 AP.

Mods

- A mods:- Quick Swap (20 points): The activation cost is reduced to 0 AP.

- B mods:- Lightweight (25 points): Reduces the Burden of this artifice by 1.
- Sniper Mode (50 points): The range of the ranged mode is increased to 240ft.
- Channeling Blasts (80 points): Your ranged mode also counts as a channeling weapon for the purpose of casting spells through it, as if the weapon it is attached to was a channeling weapon.

name: Bludgeoning Weapon (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Hollow Blow: When attacking and hitting a target, you deal additional damage if they Block. This damage is 2 on a Light/Precise attack or 4 on a Heavy attack.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Flexible Handle (10 points): When dealing damage to a target, the minimum damage you deal is always 2. (this does not trigger on damage effects if damage would be 0 otherwise).
    • Chain Flail (35 points): Cannot be defended against by 0 RP actions. Attacks made with this weapon do not count as consuming 0 RP actions.
  • B mods:
    • Weighted Poll Upsetting (25 points): When making 3 separate attacks against a target, the third attack you make with Weighted Poll Upsetting knocks the target back 10ft on damage.
    • Flanged Head (50 points): When you crit with this weapon, the damage is still maximized even against blocking targets.

name: Boar
descriptionHtml:
A boar trained to be used in combat.

 

Stats:

HP: 45

Focus: 2

Power: 4

Agility: 2

Toughness: 4

Speed: 30ft

Size: Medium

 

Special qualities: 

Boar Charge: When using the trample ability, you can trample enemies the same size as you (Medium). You also deal an additional Power damage with the attack.

Line Movement: You may only move in a straight line when taking a movement action.

name: Botany Bag
descriptionHtml:
You can carry an additional 2000 units of each Lumber material + Alchemy units.

(May be stored for 0 burden while empty)

May be crafted using Direhide / Primehide / Dragonscale / Astrahide to hold an additional 1000 / 3000 / 6000 / 10000 units of each Lumber material + Alchemy units.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • -
  • B mods:
    • One item container (50 points): The additional units this bag can carry is doubled, but it only works for a single material type rather than all of them.

name: Bow (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
High Ground: If fired against a target who is at least 10ft lower than you are, you may treat the attack as coming from the sky and calculate any low cover as if the attack originated from above the target.

Range: 240ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Recurve (20 points): Range increased by 60ft.
  • B mods:
    • Shortbow (25 points): Range reduced to 120ft but when making a basic  light attack, you can make a second light attack for 0 AP once per turn. This counts as your dual wield attack.
    • Bodkin Arrows (25 points): Grants Hollow Blow: When attacking and hitting a target, you deal additional damage if they Block. This damage is 2 on a Light/Precise attack or 4 on a Heavy attack.
    • Broadhead Arrows (50 points): Deals +1 damage against light armor or unarmored targets.
    • Blunthead Arrows (80 points): First basic heavy attack per round dazes targets on damage.

name: Breaching Flask
descriptionHtml:
A flask made for breaching locked doors. When tossed against a door, window or similar medium sized entryway it blasts them open and anyone within 5ft behind the door is knocked prone. Works against doors with a roguecraft DC equal or less than 20. Creates a loud noise regardless of success. When attempting to use it against living creatures, they may choose to avoid it for 0 RP. Else, they are knocked back 30ft.

Materials: 600 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Fire

name: Buster Weapon
descriptionHtml:
An artifice that adds an explosive blast to increase forward velocity. The most common type of weapon artifice used to help overwhelm an opponent. Attachable to melee weapons. 

Use when you are making an attack against an enemy. The attack gains +3 accuracy and +3 damage.

Can be attached for 4 AP, but once attached, can only be removed by spending an EP in town.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Powerful Explosion (10 points): Increases the damage bonus by +1
    • Optimized Explosion (20 points): Increases the accuracy bonus by +1
  • B mods:
    • Angel Type (25 points): When an attack enhanced by this artifice deals damage, knock the target back 5ft.
    • Launch Step (50 points): You can use this before making an attack to move 10ft before making an attack that benefits from this artifice.
    • Shieldbreaker (80 points): The attack enhanced by the artifice also gains the Shieldbreaker keyword.

name: Calibretto Device
descriptionHtml:
A small handheld device that display the astra level of where you're currently standing.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Bag Clip (10 points): The device can be used by attaching it to a belt or bag and no longer requires to be held in your hands.
  • B mods:
    • Item Checker (25 points): You can use the device to check objects to determine if they are corrupted with astra or safe to touch.
    • Increased Capacity (50 points): When activated, the device now remains active for a week on the same shell.
    • Radar (80 points): While active, points to the highest source of non-ambient astra within a 1 mile range.

name: Camping Kit
descriptionHtml:
Camping kits contain all of the basic tools for setting up camp.  A tent, 5 bedrolls, tinder, flint and steel, camping mat.

Rest action: You can create a comfortable camp for your party, giving you the benefits of comfortable rest (2x toughness) even in poor conditions.

name: Cannon (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Cannon: All basic attacks made with this weapon gain the Artillery keyword, but you can only make 1 ranged attack with this weapon per round. When you make a basic Heavy attack against an enemy, you make a Blanketing Light attack against all enemies within 5ft of the target as well.

Range: 240ft, reduced to 60ft if you moved before firing.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Big Boom (20 points): The light attack is made against all enemies within 10ft of the target instead of 5ft.
    • Incendiary Shell (20 points): The Light attack deals Fire damage and ignites flammable materials in its radius.
    • Mobile Artillery (35 points): After moving, your range is reduced to 120ft instead of 60ft.
  • B mods:
    • Reinforced Frame (25 points): You may use the cannon as a melee weapon that deals Physical(Bludgeoning) damage. Only does 4d6 heavy damage when used as a melee weapon.
    • Side Barrel (50 points): You may use the cannon a second time in a round, however this second attack can only be a light attack.
    • Snipe (80 points): If you did not move this turn or the previous turn you gain a +1d4 bonus to your accuracy roll on attacks made with this weapn.

Mods

name: Carpenter Materials
descriptionHtml:
Carpenter Base Materials

The base material for crafting items with carpenter is Pine. Pine units must be converted into a block before they can be used.

  • Pine:
    • Clim Cost per block: 300 Clim
    • Unit cost per block: 500 Pine units
    • Effect: None
    • When using Pine to craft an item, you must spend 1 block for a one handed weapon or cart, or 2 for a two handed weapon.

Outside of Pine, there are a variety of special materials that a carpenter can use while crafting. These are more expensive, but grant stat bonuses.

  • Maple
    • Clim Cost per block: 1050 Clim
    • Unit cost per block: 2000 Maple units.
    • Effect: +1 stat bonus
    • When using Maple to craft an item, you must spend 1 block of Maple. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional block of Pine.
  • Ash
    • Clim Cost per block: 4050 Clim
    • Unit cost per block: 8000 Ash units.
    • Effect: +1/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Ash to craft an item, you must spend 1 block of Ash. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional block of Pine.
  • Yew
    • Clim Cost per block: 8050 Clim
    • Unit cost per block: 16000 Yew units.
    • Effect: +2/+1 stat bonus
    • When using Yew to craft an item, you must spend 1 block of Yew. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional block of Pine.
  • Yggdrasil
    • Clim Cost per block: 11550 Clim
    • Unit cost per block: 23000 Yggdrasil units.
    • Effect: +2/+2 stat bonus
    • When using Yggdrasil to craft an item, you must spend 1 block of Yggdrasil. If making a two handed weapon, you must add an additional block of Pine.

Stat bonuses:

Weapon: The Material bonuses to go Focus and Power. With getting a +1 bonus or a +2/+1 bonus, the crafter may choose which of the two stats gets the extra bonus.

Carpenter Special Materials

  • Yugusbane Block (550 Clim / 15 Crafting Points) :
    • Unit cost per block:  1000 Yugusbane units.
    • Strikes with Yugusbane cause the target to be unable to teleport until the end of their next turn.
  • Silver Oil (1050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per Oil: 2000 Silver units.
    • Dealing damage with Silver infused weapons disables the Regeneration effect of Fiends until the start of your next turn.
  • Electrum Oil (3050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per Oil: 6000 Electrum units.
    • Strikes with Electrum infused weapons grant a +2 Electrum Power bonus against Divines, Summons or Otherworlders.
  • Mycelite Block (1550 Clim / 20 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per block: 3000 Mycelite units.
    • Mycelite weapons incur 1 less burden to a minimum of 1.
  • Ebony Wood Block (550 Clim)
    • Unit cost per block:  1000 Ebony Wood.
    • Ebony weapons cannot be the targets of magical effects.
  • Lignum Vitae (2050 Clim / 20 Crafting Points)
    • Unit cost per block: 4000 Lignum Vitae units
    • Lignum Vitae weapons cannot be destroyed by non Lignum Vitae tools or weapons.
  • Luxaeterna Oil (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3000 Luxaeterna units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Holy.
  • Corvuskite Oil (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3050 Corvuskite units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Dark.
  • Arcanium Oil  (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3000 Arcanium units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Arcane.
  • Vivinthal Oil  (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3000 Vivinthal units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Water.
  • Glacite Oil  (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3000 Glacite units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Frost.
  • Salachar Oil  (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3000 Salachar units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Fire.
  • Sylphsteel Oil  (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil:  3000 Sylphsteel units
    • Changes the physical damage type into Wind.
  • Fulgarite Oil ( 1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil: 3000 Fulgarite Units
    • Changes the physical damage type into Lightning.
  • Ghimslag Oil (1550 Clim / 10 Crafting points)
    • Unit cost per Oil: 3000 Ghimslag units.
    • Changes the physical damage type into Earth.
  • Nullite Oil ( 2050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per oil: 4000 Nullite units
    • All attacks made with this weapon gain the Shieldbreaker property.
  • Runesteel Oil ( 1050 Clim / 10 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per oil: 2000 Runesteel units
    • This weapon can channel spells as if it was a channeling weapon.
  • Sepsium Oil ( 2050 Clim / 30 Crafting Points):
    • Unit cost per oil: 4000 Sepsium units
    • When damaging a creature with this weapon, they cannot recover HP (except for regeneration type effects) until the start of your next turn.

name: Cart
descriptionHtml:
A simple cart used for storing items. May store an additional 2000 units worth of every material.

Must be pulled by a large creature or reduce traveling speed by half.

(Costs 1 Wood Block to craft.)

May be crafted using Maple / Ash / Yew / Yggdrasil wood to hold an additional 1000 / 3000 / 6000 / 10000 units of every material.

name: Cartographer's Kit
descriptionHtml:
Cartographer’s Kits contain a compass, gridded parchment, graphite pencils, a measuring rod, a protractor, and a travel logbook. Perfect for mapping unknown terrain and recording vital landmarks.

Effect: You can use the cartographer's kit to make maps to trade to others and can accurately mark things on the map. You may also use it to perfectly copy other maps by making an Art (Cartography) check against DC15.

An elixir that gives you a cat's agility. When drank you take 11 true damage. You gain +1 Agility. Lasts until the end of combat.

Herbs:

  • 2x Common Wind

name: Chainsaw Artifice (Two-Handed Weapon)
descriptionHtml:
Let it rip: After making an attack with this weapon, subsequent attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage.

You may consume 1 shell to empower the artifice until the end of your turn. While empowered, the damage bonus of subsequent attacks stacks.

Mods:
  • A mods:
    • Multi Gear Chaindrive (20 points): On activate. Heavy attacks grant 2 stacks instead of 1.
    • Turbo Engine (10 points): If a heavy attack is the first attack used, it grants the damage stack immediately.
  • B mods:
    • Efficient Gathering (25 points): If the artifice was used during combat, gain a +10 bonus to Gather Parts Encounter Conclusion action for that combat.
    • Redline Engine (80 points): The activation ability begins at +2 stacks, but the artifice cannot use the empower ability again after it ends until the end of the arc.

name: Counter-Magic Shackles
descriptionHtml:
Special Shackles that were created for restraining targets with high magic levels. hey disrupt the shackled target’s magic circuits, making them unable to use any magic or mana abilities. Because of the nature of the magic circuit restriction, they can only be used on unconscious targets. 

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Anti-Teleport (10 points): The wearer cannot teleport, even if the teleport would cost no mana..
  • B mods:
    • Zero Suppression (50 points): Lowers the target’s visible aura to 0 while wearing these, making them difficult to locate by magical means.

name: Crossbow (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Armor Pierce: Treats the enemy's Guard as if it was 1 lower if within 30ft of the target.

Range: 60ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Wrist-mounted (35 points): One handed crossbow only, range lowered to 30ft. Is mounted on your wrist and is always considered drawn + can hold a weapon in that hand.
    • Windlass Arbalest (35 points): Two handed crossbow only. Basic attacks with this weapon gain full pierce within 30 ft. However must be reloaded for 2 AP or 1 RP before it can be used again.
  • B mods:
    • Extended Draw (50 points): Your armor piercing effect range is increased to 60ft. If combined with a wrist mounted crossbow, increases the range but not the armor piercing range to 60ft.
    • Repeater (80 points): When you make 3 attacks with this weapon in 1 turn, you may make an additional light attack.

name: Crossbow (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Armor Pierce: Treats the enemy's Guard as if it was 2 lower if within 30ft of the target.

Range: 120ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Wrist-mounted (35 points): One handed crossbow only, range lowered to 30ft. Is mounted on your wrist and is always considered drawn + can hold a weapon in that hand.
    • Windlass Arbalest (35 points): Two handed crossbow only. Basic attacks with this weapon gain full pierce within 30 ft. However after every attack (even without the benefit), it must be reloaded for 2 AP or 1 RP before it can be used again. This attack has the augment keyword when it gains full pierce.
  • B mods:
    • Extended Draw (50 points): Your armor piercing (and arbalest full pierce) effect range is increased to 60ft.
    • Repeater (80 points): When you make 3 attacks with this weapon in 1 turn, you may make an additional light attack.

name: Culinarian Ingredients
descriptionHtml:
The base material for crafting food is Food Units.  These units can be any kind of food/filler material for example:

  • 100u of Flour
  • 50u of Fish
  • 100u of Noodles

Other than that there are special materials used for making different cooking stocks:

  • Box of Fresh Mushrooms (250c)
  • Box of Fresh Meat (250c)
  • Box of Fresh Fish (250c)
  • Box of Fresh Vegetables (250c)
  • Bundle of Spicy Peppers (250c)
  • Bundle of Fresh Cooking Herbs (250c)

In addition you can add special ingredients.

  • King Chungus Fungus (1250 Clim / 30 Crafting Points)
    • An extremely rare mushroom known for extreme deliciousness.  Gives +1 Mana Regeneration to a Stock when eating food made with the stock.

name: Culinary Kit
descriptionHtml:
Culinary Kits come with easily portable cooking knives, a pot and a pan and preserved food.  Has a special compartment to draw the pan as a weapon in a pinch!  The included pan isn't tough enough for a skilled chef, but good enough for a novice.

Rest Action: You cook a meal for your party at the cost of 40 units of food (equal to 20 Clim) per person, giving you the benefits of food (2 mana) for the rest.

name: Demonblood Potion
descriptionHtml:
Not actually made out of Demonblood, it refers to Demon tenacity.  The potion is an intense stimulant that allows a character to fight through pain and injury. When drank, you ignore the penalties of one injury until the end of the encounter.

Crafting:
Materials: 250 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Arcane
  • 1x Rare Fire

name: Diamondize Potion
descriptionHtml:
Temporarily encases the user in hard crystal, rendering them nearly impervious to damage but incapacitating them in stasis. While in stasis, you cannot take any actions but all damage (including true damage) you take is reduced by 200. Lasts for 10 minutes and can be used while downed.

Crafting:
Materials: 4400 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Supreme Earth
  • 1x Mystic Arcane
  • 1x Mystic Water

name: Displacement Potion
descriptionHtml:
When drunk, you teleport 40ft in a random direction (roll 1d8 to determine this, 1 being north and 5 being south etc). If this would place you inside a wall or otherwise occupied place, the teleport fails and the potion is wasted.

Crafting:
Materials: 350 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Wind
descriptionText:
When drunk, you teleport 40ft in a random direction (roll 1d8 to determine this, 1 being north and 5 being south etc). If this would place you inside a wall or otherwise occupied place, the teleport fails and the potion is wasted.

Crafting:
Materials: 350 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

- 1x Rare Wind

name: Dueling Weapon (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Dueling: When attacking a single enemy during a turn at least 3 times with this weapon, your next Dodge or Block against that enemy costs 0 RP until the start of your next turn. This property cannot be used if you target any other enemy during your turn or if you spend AP making a Heavy attack against the target.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Spade Lunga (10 points): When moving at least 40ft towards an enemy, increases the range of your next attack made with this weapon by 5 ft.
    • Ornate Blade (35 points): Moving into the target’s threatened zone the first time in a turn counts as 1 attack for the requirement to activate Dueling.
  • B mods:
    • Paired Duelers (25 points): When wielding two dueling weapons with paired duelers, your offhand extra attack counts as one attack for your dueling bonus on your main hand weapon.
    • Passato Sotto (80 points): You may make 1 Heavy attack with this weapon and still gain the dueling effect.
    • Ferrobri Grip (80 points):  Dueling weapon passive changes to Aggressive Dueling: When attacking a single enemy 3 times with this weapon in 1 turn, your next basic attack with this weapon this turn has its cost reduced by 1 AP. Once per round.

name: Eagle Potion
descriptionHtml:
When drunk, you can assess the status of a single enemy until the end of combat. This functions like the Status Assessment II ability.

Crafting:
Materials: 520 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Wind
  • 1x Common Fire

name: Elemental Grease
descriptionHtml:
An flask that when splashed over your weapon changes the damage it deals to the element of the flask until the end of the encounter.

There exist flasks for Arcane, Fire, Water, Frost, Lightning, Wind, Earth, Holy and Dark damage.

Crafting:
Materials: 350 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare herb of matching element (Arcane for holy/dark, Wind for Lightning, Water for Frost)

name: Elemental Immunity Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that raises your resistance against a type of damage. When drunk, you take 11 true damage. You gain 10 damage reduction against a single type of damage, determined by the type of elixir. This lasts until the end of the encounter. 

There exist elixirs for Arcane, Fire, Water, Frost, Lightning, Wind, Earth, Holy and Dark resistance.

Crafting:
Materials: 1250 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 3x Rare herb of matching element (Arcane for holy/dark, Wind for Lightning, Water for Frost)

name: Elemental Mixture Flask
descriptionHtml:
A flask that when thrown explodes in a 15x15ft area dealing damage based on the type of flask. May be thrown up to 40ft away. You may make a light attack against all caught in the explosion area. The damage type of this is determined by the type of flask. There exist flasks that deal Arcane, Fire, Water, Frost, Wind, Lightning, Earth, Holy and Dark damage.

Crafting:
Materials: 0 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare herb of the element (Arcane for holy/dark, Wind for Lightning, Water for Frost)

name: Elemental Weapon Artifice
descriptionHtml:
An artifice that sends energy through the weapon. The type is predetermined and can not be changed. The appearance and effect are based on the type. For example, a fire weapon might cover the weapon in flames and deal fire damage. 

When your attacks with this weapon deal damage, the type of damage they deal changes based on this weapon’s element. This effect lasts for 1 minute. Can only be applied to melee weapons.

Can be attached or detached from a melee weapon by spending 4 AP.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Early Deactivation (10 points): You may spend 0 RP to deactivate the effect.
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced to 0 AP.
  • B mods:
    • Secondary Element (50 points): The artifice is made with two predetermined elements instead of 1. When activating it, you may choose which element is used.
    • Ranged Mode (50 points): This artifice can be attached to ranged weapons as well.
    • Elemental Burst (80 points): While active, you may expend the shell and 1 AP to make a Heavy attack with the weapon.
    • Elemental Mastery (80): When activated, the artifice instead changes to an element the wielder has mastered (their choosing). Still has the base element too as option.

name: Equipment Patching Kit
descriptionHtml:
Contains all the necessary materials to jury rig a fix on damaged weapons.

Rest Action: You can patch a broken piece of equipment, allowing it to be usable until the end of the scenario if it is repairable. The equipment still counts as broken at the end of the scenario and cannot be patched again until fully repaired.

name: Eye Lens
descriptionHtml:
A lens that boosts the power of mystic eyes when crafted with good materials. Has little effect normally, except for looking stylish.

If crafted with Tamahagane, Mithril, Orihalcum or Escudo gives a bonus to Mystic Eye attacks as if it was a weapon. 

This item is crafted similarly to an artifice weapon and gives the same sort of Material bonus, but cannot take alloys, universal mods or burden reducing effects.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Zoom In (20 points): Can function like binoculars. 
  • B mods:
    • Focused Lens(25 points): When you deal damage with a mystic eye ability, you make immediately make a Light attack against one of the targets you damaged with it. This counts as your dual wield attack.
    • Silver Lens (80 points): Mystic Eye attacks made with this lens count as silver.

name: Falcon Elixir
descriptionHtml:
When drunk, you take 11 true damage. You gain the ability to fly with a speed equal to your normal speed. The effect lasts for 10 rounds.

Crafting:
Materials: 1250 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Wind
  • 1x Rare Arcane

name: Farming Materials
descriptionHtml:
Seeds:

There are several basic seeds farmers can use:

  • Food Units:
    • Cost: 5 Clim
    • Difficulty: 5
    • Yields: 150 Food units
    • Growing time: 1
  • Alchemy Units:
    • Cost: 5 Clim
    • Difficulty: 5
    • Yields: 150 Alchemy units
    • Growing time: 1
  • Alchemy Herbs:
    • Cost: 10 Clim
    • Difficulty: 5
    • Yields: 1 common herb of the element of the seed
    • Growing time: 1

Cost is the value of the seed.

Difficulty is how much crafting points it takes to gather the yield when harvesting.

Yield is what the seed gives once harvested.

Growing time is how long a seed takes to be harvested. A growing time of 1 means it can be harvested immediately once you have enough IP to use the Harvest ability. When a seed has a growing time longer than 1, it takes harvests equal to the growing time to receive the yield. When resolving a harvest with a seed with a growing time of more than 1 remaining, you still need to pay the difficulty to take care of the seed like normal.

Special Seeds:

These function like normal seeds, but are not typically available in stores.

  • Moon-Phase Seeds:
    • Cost: 25 Clim
    • Difficulty 15
    • Yield: 1 rare herb of the element of the seed
    • Growing time 1
    • Special: If you let it grow for another season after the first, the yield changes to a Mystic Herb of the same element.
Fertilizer:
  • Average:
    • Cost: 250 Clim
    • Effect: +1 Crafting dice
  • Good
    • Cost: 500 Clim
    • Effect: +2 Crafting dice
  • Excellent:
    • Cost: 1000 Clim
    • Effect: +3 Crafting Dice
  • Supreme:
    • Cost: 2000 Clim
    • Effect: +4 Crafting Dice

Fertilizer can be applied during the harvesting part. You can only apply 1 fertilizer at the same time.

name: Fire Robe
descriptionHtml:
A red robe with a design based of a Divine Arms. Keeps the wearer cool in even the hottest of weathers by repelling the heat.

Can be activated to give the wearer 5 damage reduction against fire damage until the end of the encounter. When this effect ends, the shell inside the robe is immediately used up and needs to be replaced for the weather repelling effect to function.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced to 0 AP.
  • B mods:
    • Fireproof (25 points): While it's active, you are immune to Burning.
    • Increased Protection (80 points): The damage reduction is increased to 10.

descriptionHtml:
An Alchemical mix that creates an area of flame. May be thrown up to 40ft away. Creates a 15x15ft area of fire that lasts until the start of your next turn. Any creature ending their turn in the area takes 20 full pierce fire damage.

  • 1x Common Fire

name: Gauntlets (One-handed)
descriptionHtml:
Unarmed Combat: Attacks with this weapon count as unarmed attacks for the purposes of Unarmed Strike. If you are proficient in unarmed attacks, you are automatically proficient in this weapon. Otherwise, you are never proficient in this weapon.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Simple Design (10 points): You are considered proficient with these even if you normally would not be.
    • Red Gauntlets (20 points): For every AP spent on basic movement, you gain +2 damage to your next attack with this weapon (up to a maximum of +4 dmg).
    • Grip Design (20 points): You may wield a Two-Handed weapon with the hand this gauntlet is on. Any other actions that require a free hand normally such as grabbing a potion not doable.
    • Martial Mastery (35 points): You may have your attacks deal Elemental damage instead of physical based on your mastered classes. Bajiquan for Fire, Monk for Water, Sky Talon for Wind and Still Stone for Earth, Transcendent Lightning for Lightning, Five Harmonies for Holy, Black Oath for Dark.
  • B mods:
    • Brown Gauntlets (25 points): While wearing this item, you gain a +4 Shield bonus to your block. This stacks with Still Stone Style's Shielding Fist, but not with other shields.
    • Wrist Claw (50 points): While wearing this item, you may still hold an item in that hand as if it was empty.
    • Katar (50 points): Grants Nimble. Nimble: After attacking an enemy with a light attack on your turn, you may move 5ft. This movement only provokes attacks of opportunity if that movement would put you out of melee range of the target.
    • Black Gauntlets (80 points): When dealing damage with this weapon, you may take true damage equal to the damage dice (ie 6 on a d6) to maximize the damage of one die.

name: Ghostblade
descriptionHtml:
Current Wielder: Unknown

A weapon that mimics the movement and attacks of ghostly creatures.

 

Level 1:
For your first 20ft of movement during a turn, you become Ethereal. You do not provoke attacks of opportunity and may move through solid objects.

 

Level 2:
Once per arc, you may use Soul Pierce

 

Soul Pierce
Cost: 2 AP
Keywords: Sure Hit, Full Pierce
Range: Melee Weapon Range
Requirement: -
Description: 
You may make a Precise attack against the target that deals maximum Heavy damage and lowers the target’s mana by 5. If the target has 0 or less mana after this, they take an additional maximum Heavy damage.

name: Giant Dragonfly
descriptionHtml:
A dragonfly trained to be used in combat.

 

Stats:

HP: 18

Focus: 3

Power: 2

Agility: 6

Toughness: 1

Speed: 30ft

Size: Small

 

Special Qualities:

Tiny Flying Mount: Your mount can fly. While moving only on the ground, your speed is reduced to 5ft. You may use the cavalier’s ability Rider & Mount As One to re-direct attacks away from your mount even while flying. Any damage you take while using this will cause you to lose altitude as if your mount took damage.

Spectral Trample: You may apply the trample effect to creatures of any size, but your attack does not gain Sure Hit, displace the enemy or knock them prone. If they dodge your attack, your trample movement is not canceled.

name: Giant Scissors (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Giant Scissors are used by Foragers in their activities.  With proficiency, they count as a 2h weapon that can be used as a foraging tool.

Shearing: You deal +1 damage against unarmored targets. 

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Elegant Executioner (35 points): Reduces Vivisect AP cost by 1.
    • Diamond Tip (35 points): Shearing now always deals +1 damage regardless of armor.
  • B mods:
    • Executioner's Blade (50 points): Heavy basic attacks strike an adjacent target in range with a light attack. Once per round.
    • Separation (80 points): You can separate the Giant Scissors into two pieces for 0 AP (Quick), allowing you to dual wield them. You can use Separation again to put them back together. The passive changes from Shearing to Shearing Flurry which instead grants +1 accuracy to unarmored targets.
descriptionText:
