name: Gourmet Cooking Stock
descriptionHtml:
Can be added to meals to make gourmet meals.  When cooking food during rest actions, you can create gourmet food which grants non-stacking buffs.  Can only be used when cooking fresh food with a Culinary Kit or a Kitchen.  Cooking stocks typically come in a pack of 10 and it uses one cooking stock per meal.  If you are cooking a big meal for your party members, you need one stock per serving.  When using the Culinary Kit action with a stock, you must have enough stock to cover all of the food being made, otherwise the effect does not work.  For example, if you are cooking for 4 people, you need 160u of food and 4 cooking stocks to get the cooking stock effect.  (Remember, you can only use your culinary kit once per rest).

To make a cooking stock you start with a base, which requires 500u of any mix of food. 

  • Mushroom Stock (+Focus)
  • Meaty Stock (+Power)
  • Fish Stock (+Toughness)
  • Vegetable Stock (+Agility)
  • Spicy Stock (+Crafting)
  • Herbal Stock (+Gathering)

Culinarians can make multi-flavored stocks as well, which can increase more than one attribute at once.

When using a Gourmet stock, the meal counts as a gourmet meal and gives +1 mana.

When crafting a stock, you make 10 at a time.

Using stock from scratch example:

  1. Obtain 500u of Mushrooms.
  2. Errand action to create a box of fresh mushrooms.
  3. Craft Base Stock for 500u of any food.
  4. Change base stock to mushroom stock by putting in the box of fresh mushrooms and using the Culinarian skill.
  5. Use Stir Layered Flavors to add another ingredient.  Adding a box of fresh meat to make a Meaty Mushroom stock.
  6. Use Every Last Drop to increase stock yield by 2.
  7. You obtain 10+2 Meaty Mushroom Stock which grants +1 Focus and +1 Power Food Bonus for 24 hours.
  8. During a rest action, cooking for 5 people, you add 5 Meaty Mushroom Stock and 200u of food to make 5 Meaty Mushroom Dishes to feed your party and give them buffs.
descriptionText:
Can be added to meals to make gourmet meals.  When cooking food during rest actions, you can create gourmet food which grants non-stacking buffs.  Can only be used when cooking fresh food with a Culinary Kit or a Kitchen.  Cooking stocks typically come in a pack of 10 and it uses one cooking stock per meal.  If you are cooking a big meal for your party members, you need one stock per serving.  When using the Culinary Kit action with a stock, you must have enough stock to cover all of the food being made, otherwise the effect does not work.  For example, if you are cooking for 4 people, you need 160u of food and 4 cooking stocks to get the cooking stock effect.  (Remember, you can only use your culinary kit once per rest).

To make a cooking stock you start with a base, which requires 500u of any mix of food.

- Mushroom Stock (+Focus)
- Meaty Stock (+Power)
- Fish Stock (+Toughness)
- Vegetable Stock (+Agility)
- Spicy Stock (+Crafting)
- Herbal Stock (+Gathering)
Culinarians can make multi-flavored stocks as well, which can increase more than one attribute at once.

When using a Gourmet stock, the meal counts as a gourmet meal and gives +1 mana.

When crafting a stock, you make 10 at a time.

Using stock from scratch example:

- Obtain 500u of Mushrooms.
- Errand action to create a box of fresh mushrooms.
- Craft Base Stock for 500u of any food.
- Change base stock to mushroom stock by putting in the box of fresh mushrooms and using the Culinarian skill.
- Use Stir Layered Flavors to add another ingredient.  Adding a box of fresh meat to make a Meaty Mushroom stock.
- Use Every Last Drop to increase stock yield by 2.
- You obtain 10+2 Meaty Mushroom Stock which grants +1 Focus and +1 Power Food Bonus for 24 hours.
- During a rest action, cooking for 5 people, you add 5 Meaty Mushroom Stock and 200u of food to make 5 Meaty Mushroom Dishes to feed your party and give them buffs.

name: Grapeshot Flask
descriptionHtml:
An Alchemical mix that explodes when shattered, sending small metal balls in all directions. May be thrown up to 40ft away. All targets within a 15x15ft area take 5 full pierce physical damage.

Materials: 180 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Fire

name: Grappling Hook Artifice
descriptionHtml:
A handheld cylinder shaped artifice that fires out a pin with a 40ft rope attached to it. The pin attaches itself to any non-living surface it hits and it can support the weight of up to four people. The GM may require an accuracy check for distant or hard to hit surfaces.

After the pin is attached, you may spend 0 AP to reel yourself in and move towards where the pin is attached to. Any allies within 5ft of you may grab onto you and be moved alongside you for 0 RP (Up to 3 allies total).

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Drive By Model (10 points): The Grappling Hook Artifice loses the ability to move allies, but you can now make attacks at any point while being reeled as long as you have actions. While you are moving in this way, you do not provoke attacks of opportunity.
    • Scorpion Tip (20 points): The range of the Grappling Hook Artifice is lowered to 20ft, but can be used to make a light attack that does exactly 1 damage with full pierce. On damage, the target can be pulled up to 20ft towards the user.
    • Swing Engine (35 points): The grappling hook artifice can be used to swing from the point the pin is embedded instead of pulling straight towards it, in addition the rope length is increased by 20ft. The pin automatically detaches and reels in when you move to your destination for smooth traveling.
  • B mods:
    • Emergency Escape Reel (25 points): You may also fire this using 1 RP.
    • Heavy Tow Line (25 points): The grappling hook can support 1 additional ally to move with you.
    • Belt Attachment (50 points): The Grappling Hook Artifice is modified to be mounted onto the belt, allowing for operation while wielding items in both hands via a switch mounted on the arm.
    • Safety Hook (80 points): You and your allies movement with this artifice is considered a sidestep.

name: Heavy Blade (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Overpower: When you land a critical hit with a Heavy Blade, you deal an additional 1d4 damage if it was a light attack or precise attack and an additional 1d12 damage if it was a heavy attack. This extra damage is not automatically maximized from rolling a critical hit, but you keep the extra damage even if the target blocked the critical hit.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Overwhelming Edge (20 points): You cannot be disarmed or parried.
    • Wide Blade (35 points): When you are wielding this in two hands and don’t make an attack with this weapon on your turn you can grant yourself or an adjacent ally low cover against ranged attacks for 0 RP until the start of your next turn. Can only use this once per round.
  • B mods:
    • Quakemaker (25 points): Critical hits render the target prone regardless of if they hit or not.
    • Gutsy Blade (50 points): The crit threshold of this weapon is lowered by 5, but you no longer maximize damage dice or gain full pierce on a crit.
    • Executioner's Blade (50 points): Heavy basic attacks strike an adjacent target in range with a light attack. Once per round.
    • Unreasonable Mass (80 points): Heavy attacks gain 1 additional dice in damage (5d6->6d6) but suffer -1 accuracy on all attacks.

name: Hori
descriptionHtml:
Hori knives are used by Foragers in their activities.  With proficiency, they count as small weapons that can be used as a foraging tool.


Hidden Weapon: You may draw this weapon for 0 AP. Each combat, after you draw the weapon if the next attack you make that round is using this weapon and is a basic Light or Heavy attack, it gains the Trick Attack keyword. After benefiting from this bonus, you cannot do so again the same combat even if drawing a different weapon with this ability.


Mods

  • A mods:
    • Aerodynamic Design (10 points): Boomerang/Return effects cannot be used with this. For 1 RP you can throw the dagger, making a light ranged attack.  On damage, the target is slowed until the end of its next turn.
    • Compact Ergonomics (20 points): When your other hand is free and you take a movement action that costs RP, that movement range is increased by 5ft. 
    • Parrying Dagger (20 points): If you don’t use this weapon to make an attack during your turn, you gain a +4 bonus to your Block until the start of your next turn. You also gain a +4 bonus to your ‘damage’ roll when using the Parry ability.
    • Flat Pommel (35 points): When your other hand is free, you use your free hand to push the target into targets increasing melee heavy attack damage to 5d6.
  • B mods:
    • Poisoner Weapon (25 points): Applying poison to this weapon costs -1 AP (min 0).
    • Butterfly Knife (50 points): Your first light attack each round with this weapon gains Trick Attack, but it can no longer make Heavy attacks.
    • Ring Pommel (50 points): If you make a heavy attack that the target defends against, you do not trigger attacks of opportunity from them until the start of your next turn.

name: Katana (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Keen: You score a critical hit on a 19 or 20.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Mastercrafted Saya (20 points): The first single target attack made with this weapon per combat also draws the weapon simultaneously.  This first attack has its crit threshold lowered by 1.
    • Sori Curve (20 points): When you roll a 1 on an accuracy roll, you can reroll the attack. Your target(s) can make an opportunity attack, even with a ranged weapon.  This opportunity attack does not provoke other opportunity attacks.
    • Beneri Special (35 points): Grants a +1/+1 Bonus, doesn’t stack with material bonuses. Weapon shatters, becoming irreparably broken on a 1 roll for an accuracy roll.
  • B mods:
    • Equilibrium Forging (25 points): You do not roll damage on this weapon, but deal slightly higher than average damage. Instead it deals 6 damage on light attacks or 15 damage (1h) or 19 damage (2h). Critical strikes apply maximum damage as normal.
    • Hamon Line (50 points): Grants Keen II lowers the crit threshold by 2 (replaces Keen).
    • Swallow's Edge (80 points): The crit threshold decreases by 1 for each attack made this turn with a Swallow’s Edge weapon. This bonus resets if you land a critical hit. If both your mainhand and offhand weapon have this mod, the effect aplies to both weapons at the same time.

name: Lance (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Charger Weapon: Can only be used while mounted. Counts as a polearm weapon and has all the same properties of one. Your first attack made with this weapon type after moving at least 20ft deal maximum damage instead of rolling.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Crown Tip (10 points): The first basic attack per round made immediately after moving 50ft or more, dismounts mounted targets on damage.
    • Demi-Lance (20 points): Can be used while dismounted as a normal spear, but loses its movement max damage bonus unless mounted.
  • B mods:
    • Justice Lance (50 points): Your movement speed is capped at 25ft while this is in your burden. This weapon has an additional 5ft range and a 15ft threaten range.
    • Shield Lance (80 points): When spending at least 3 AP to move in one turn, your next basic defense before the start of your next turn costs 1 less RP.

  • 1x Mystic Earth
  • 1x Mystic Arcane

name: Light Sword (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Nimble: After attacking an enemy with a light attack on your turn, you may move 5ft. This movement only provokes attacks of opportunity if that movement would put you out of melee range of the target.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Single Edge (20 points): Nimble instead becomes Nimble Rotation. After attacking you can no longer move away from the enemy, but you can sidestep to anywhere adjacent to the enemy taking the most direct path possible when making a melee attack. This cannot be used if the target is reduced to 0 HP from the attack.
    • Heroic Emblem (20 points): Grants a +1 power or +1 focus bonus, but cannot gain the benefits from base material bonuses. This bonus is actually 100% from hype.
    • Fullering Groves (35 points): Gives Nimble II. After attacking an enemy with a light attack on your turn, you may move 10ft.  This movement only provokes attacks of opportunity if that movement would put you out of melee range of the target.
  • B mods:
    • Extended Quillons (25 points):  Grants +1 to guard when out of RP.
    • Weighted Edge (25 points): You may use Nimble (and Nimble II or Nimble Rotation etc if you have those instead) even after making a Heavy or Precise attack.
    • Gladius Edge (50 points): When you are struck by a critical hit and you do not defend against it, you can make a 0 RP opportunity attack before the triggering attack. You can spend 1 RP to instead make it a heavy attack.
    • Axebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Axebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding axes.

- B mods:- Extended Quillons (25 points):  Grants +1 to guard when out of RP.
- Weighted Edge (25 points): You may use Nimble (and Nimble II or Nimble Rotation etc if you have those instead) even after making a Heavy or Precise attack.
- Gladius Edge (50 points): When you are struck by a critical hit and you do not defend against it, you can make a 0 RP opportunity attack before the triggering attack. You can spend 1 RP to instead make it a heavy attack.
- Axebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Axebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding axes.

name: Lightning Rod
descriptionHtml:
A short metal rod that can be expanded with the push of a button.

When placed into the ground and activated, it creates a 25x25ft zone with it in the center. Everyone in the zone gains 5 damage reduction against Lightning damage. Lasts until the end of the encounter.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Telescopic Pole (20 points): The zone increases to 30x30ft.
    • Quick Activation (35 points): The activation cost is reduced by 1.
  • B mods:
    • Shockproof (25 points): While it's active, everyone in the area also gains immunity to Shock.
    • Increased Protection (80 points): The damage reduction is increased to 10.

name: Lizard Elixir
descriptionHtml:
When drunk with a severed limb held to the right part of the body, the limb will begin to re-attach. Has to be drunk within 5 minutes of the loss. After drinking, you become stunned for a minute until the process finishes.

Crafting:
Materials: 1400 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Water
  • 1x Rare Earth
descriptionText:

name: Locking Seal
descriptionHtml: A piece of paper inscribed with a single Kiraran character of ‘Lock’. When placed on a door, chest or similar object it prevents any person from opening it. The person who placed it can safely remove it, but for everyone else it counts as a tier 1 bounded field. Destroying the seal itself through physical force is impossible by normal means, but if the object it's placed on is sufficiently damaged, then the seal is broken.

Destroying the seal itself through physical force is impossible by normal means, but if the object it's placed on is sufficiently damaged, then the seal is broken.

name: Long Distance Communications Array
descriptionHtml: A series of boxes, microphones and earplugs that is used for long range communications. It can connect to other devices by inputting a code unique to the artifice its connecting to. Any sound spoken into the microphone is broadcasted to all other artifices connected to it. It has unlimited range.
descriptionText: A series of boxes, microphones and earplugs that is used for long range communications. It can connect to other devices by inputting a code unique to the artifice its connecting to. Any sound spoken into the microphone is broadcasted to all other artifices connected to it. It has unlimited range.

name: Longsword (One-Handed/Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Versatile: At the start of your turn, before you make any attacks you may choose to treat this as either a One-Handed weapon or a Two-Handed weapon. When using a longsword as a One-Handed weapon and no other weapon or shield, you may treat the longsword as a shield as well. When using it as a Two-Handed Weapon, you deal an additional 1d4 damage on a critical hit. This extra damage is not automatically maximized from rolling a critical hit, but you keep the extra damage even if the target blocked the critical hit.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Catch Pommel (10 points): When using the Longsword with one hand, once per round you can extend the range of your attack by 5ft.
    • War Sword (20 points): Your first basic attack made with this weapon after moving at least 20ft maximizes one damage dice of your choice instead of rolling.
    • Bastard Sword (35 points): You gain a +1 Armorbreaker Power bonus against targets in Heavy armor or higher.
  • B mods:
    • Extended Quillons (25 points): Grants +1 to guard when out of RP.
    • Heavy Crossguard (25 points): You retain the Block bonus even while using the weapon with two hands, the block value increases by +4 when wielding with one hand and no other weapon or shield in your other hand.
    • Lange Messer (50 points): Grants +1 to guard but increases burden by +1.
    • Axebreaker (80 points):  You gain a +2 Axebreaker Power bonus against targets wielding axes.

name: Mage's Blood
descriptionHtml:
Contact poison. Can be applied to a weapon. Lasts for 1 hit or 1 minute (6 rounds).

When an attack with this poison damages an enemy, they are inflicted with a Circuit Crash debuff. The next time they use mana before the end of the encounter, they take 4d6+8 damage.

Discovered by Beetle Swallower.

Crafting:
Materials: 1800 Alchemy Materials
Special Materials: 

  • 3x vial of Heartwood Poison Water.
descriptionText:
Contact poison. Can be applied to a weapon. Lasts for 1 hit or 1 minute (6 rounds).

When an attack with this poison damages an enemy, they are inflicted with a Circuit Crash debuff. The next time they use mana before the end of the encounter, they take 4d6+8 damage.

name: Mana Vision Goggles
descriptionHtml:
A pair of goggles that link themselves to the wielder’s eyes. Once put on, they are always active. They allow the wielder to see the fluctuations of mana, letting them see the presence of any magic or mana based abilities including traps or barriers. 

They can also automatically detect the presence of any creature that has mana that is not behind total cover. (This does not give you vision to attack them if they would be in things such as glittershards). However, they become unable to detect living creatures without mana in them.

Due to the nature of these goggles, they must be carefully removed. Removing them takes a minute. Attempting to do it faster will blind the person wielding them for a minute instead.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Mana Sight (20 points): Looking at a target within 30ft automatically reveals their mana %.
  • B mods:
    • Quick Removal (50 points): Can be removed by spending 3 AP instead of 1 minute.
    • Decipher Magic (80 points): When looking at a magic effect, you may attempt to understand it as if using Decipher Magic. Your bonus for this roll is +15, regardless of your reason or magic skill points.

name: Marlone Jelly Poison
descriptionHtml:
Contact Poison. Can be applied to a weapon. Lasts for 1 hits or 1 minute (6 rounds).

When an attack with this poison damages a grunt type enemy, the target is stunned until the end of this next turn. An enemy can only be affected by this poison once per combat.

Crafting:
Materials: 700 Alchemy Materials
Special Materials:

  • 1x Rare Water Herb

name: Medical Kit
descriptionHtml:
Medical Kits contain basic bandages, salves, alcohol, scissors and a knife.  Perfect for patching up light wounds.

Rest action: You heal yourself or an ally for their Toughness+5.  A character can only be affected once per rest by Medical Kit.

Rest action: You heal yourself or an ally for their Toughness+5.  A character can only be affected once per rest by Medical Kit.

name: Medium Shell
descriptionHtml: A medium cylinder of magical fuel that is used to power artifices.  Most weapon-type artifices automatically eject the shell when they are emptied… along with a burst of steam to prevent overheating.
descriptionText: A medium cylinder of magical fuel that is used to power artifices.  Most weapon-type artifices automatically eject the shell when they are emptied… along with a burst of steam to prevent overheating.

name: Memory Bubble
descriptionHtml:
A spherical object that can record memories. A person can record up to 1, 2 or 5 minutes of memory into the object depending on the type. After a memory is recorded onto this artifice, no further memories can be recorded even if the time is not fully spent.

After a memory is recorded, other people can view the memory. When using this object, they view the memory from the point of view of the recorder.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Infinite Replays (35 points): A person may watch the recording by spending 1 mana rather than using a shell.
  • B mods:
    • Redacted (25 points): The recorder can mute or censor parts of the memory being recorded. This tampering may fool regular people, but an artifice check of DC20 will reveal tampering has been done.

name: Military Ration
descriptionHtml:
A ready to eat meal.  Grants the effects of food for rest without having to cook or prepare anything. Can be consumed during a rest to gain the effects of food (2 mana).

Typically consists of of dried meats, dried fruits and blocks of compressed grains with honey.  Sometimes includes vials of sauce.  Typically pressed together and then rolled in linen cloth, but higher end ones can come in wooden boxes.

If eaten during an encounter, it's a Setup action that takes 8 AP to complete.

name: Mimic Potion
descriptionHtml:
When drunk, your appearance shifts to that of an average sized chest. Lasts up to a minute or until a character attempts to interact with you.

Crafting:
Materials: 360 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Arcane
  • 1x Common Earth

name: Mining Bag
descriptionHtml:
You can carry an additional 2000 units of each mining material.

(May be stored for 0 burden while empty)

May be crafted using Direhide / Primehide / Dragonscale / Astrahide to hold an additional 1000 / 3000 / 6000 / 10000 units of each mining material.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • -
  • B mods:
    • One item container (50 points): The additional units this bag can carry is doubled, but it only works for a single material type rather than all of them.

name: Minor Item
descriptionHtml:
Generic listing for minor items not listed separately. May contain any item within reason (GM's discretion), but should only contain adventuring or daily life utility stuff.

For example: Shovel, Rope, Ink, Bedroll, Sack, Piton etc

name: Mule
descriptionHtml:
A mule used for pulling carts and storing items. Can't be used as mount in combat (Abilities like Trample will not work if mounted on a mule).

May store an additional 1000 units worth of every material.

Stats:

HP: 20

Focus: 3

Power: 3

Agility: 3

Toughness: 3

Speed: 20ft

Size: Large

name: Musket (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Superior Armor Pierce: Treats the enemy’s Guard as if it was 1 lower.

Range: 120ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Range Finder (20 points): You add a range finder to the weapon. This does not increase its range, but does allow you to look through the lens for a +10 perception bonus against things further than 60ft away. In addition, you can tell how far objects are from you when doing this.
    • Bayonet (35 points): You may make melee attacks with this weapon that deal Physical(Piercing) damage without provoking attacks of opportunity. These attacks don't do 2h melee damage.
  • B mods:
    • Long Barrel (25 points): Your weapon gains 240ft range, but you don’t armor pierce when shooting at targets further than 120ft away.

name: Netting Flask
descriptionHtml:
A flask that when thrown explodes in a 5ft radius around the target and conjures up magnetic nets. The target and everyone within 5ft of it become Rooted until the start of your next turn or until they use 2 AP to remove the net. May be thrown up to 40ft away

Crafting:
Materials: 1950 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Wind
  • 1x Rare Earth

name: Night Vision Goggles
descriptionHtml:
Special goggles that grant the user night vision up to 120ft in addition to keeping the eyes protected from weather and impacts. Easy to operate with the flick of a switch.  

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Anti-Flash Shutter (20 points): While this artifice is worn and active, you are protected from being dazed, blinded etc by sudden flashes of light.
    • Increased Range (20 points): Increases the range at which these goggles are active by 60ft.
  • B mods:
    • Darkvision Goggles (50 points): Instead of Nightvision, these goggles give you Darkvision.
    • Thermal Fusion Sight (80 points): Adds thermal vision up to 120ft. This cannot detect things hidden by magical means. The sight can not discern a difference in heat once something reaches freezing temperatures.

name: Northi Lantern
descriptionHtml:
A contained magical light within a casing. The light can be easily turned on and off with the flick of a switch and provides clear and stable white light that emits up to 30 feet away.  

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Light control (10 points): The distance of light emitted can be any distance up to the normal max.
    • Directional Light (20 points): Makes the light directionally forward instead of all around.
  • B mods:
    • Lumen Sphere (25 points): Increases the light emission to 60ft.
    • Bag Clip (25 points): Can be attached to your belt or bag to be used without being held in your hand.

name: Parrot Elixir
descriptionHtml:
When drunk, your appearance shifts to that of a random, average looking person of the same gender and race. This lasts for 24 hours. If drunk again before the time is up, the duration is extended by 24 hours.

Crafting:
Materials: 560 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Wind
  • 3x Common Arcane

name: Pegasus
descriptionHtml:
A pegasus trained to be used in combat.

 

Stats:

HP: 45

Focus: 4

Power: 4

Agility: 5

Toughness: 4

Speed: 60ft

Size: Large

 

Special qualities:

Flying Mount: Your mount can fly. While flying you may only move in a straight line when making a movement action. While moving only on the ground, your speed is reduced to 20ft but this restriction does not apply.

In flight vulnerability: When your mount takes damage while flying, its height is lowered by 20ft. If this would reduce its height to the ground level, it becomes rooted and cannot fly again until the end of your next turn. If the attack that reduced your HP came from a flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude.

name: Personal Camouflage Field
descriptionHtml:
A circular device that barely fits in one hand. When activated, the user’s appearance shifts the match the surroundings. The user is able to hide without cover with a +10 bonus to stealth checks from any creature that was unaware of its presence before. In combat or against creatures looking for the wearer, the cloak will only give a +5 bonus to stealth checks.

Upon making an attack, the effect of this artifice is disabled until the start of your next turn.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Vanishing Act (20 points): When you activate the device in combat, you may immediately take the Hide action for -2 AP of its normal cost (min 0).
    • Group Veil (35 points): While stationary, you may extend the effect of this device to all medium or smaller creatures within 5ft of you.
  • B mods:
    • Decoy (50 points): The first time you are attacked while stealthed while this device is active, you automatically dodge the attack.
    • Thermal Mask (80 points): Thermal vision and similar heat based detection no longer automatically detects you.

name: Photon Shield
descriptionHtml:
A bracelet that projects a shield of light onto the users arm. Activates a projected shield of light onto your arm, which acts as a shield but does not use your hand to wield it allowing you to carry a weapon in it.  Does not stack with other shields and while activated, it is treated as a shield for things with that as requirement.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced to 0 AP
    • Photon Dome (20 points): Changes the Photon Shield effect to instead project a dome 5ft around you that grants the shield effect to allies against ranged attacks only.  Does not stack with other equipped shields. Does not stack with other Photon modifications. May still choose to activate the original effect of Photon Shield.
    • Photon Wall (20 points): Changes the Photon Shield effect to instead to create a stationary wall of light in front of you. This wall disappears when you move. The wall is a 1 way high cover with 20 HP and only allows ranged attacks to pass through it from your side. When you move, the wall is immediately destroyed. Once the wall is destroyed, the shell is expended and ejects from the artifice. May still choose to activate the original effect of Photon Shield.
  • B mods:
    • Photon Greatshield (25 points): Increases the Burden to 2 and changes the effect of Photon Shield to a Greatshield instead of a regular shield. Reduces your evasion etc like a Greatshield would. You can deactivate the photon greatshield for 0 RP, but doing so ejects the shell and ends the effect.

name: Pickaxe (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Pickaxes are used by Miners in their activities. With proficiency, they are treated as a One-Handed axe that can be used as a miner's tool.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Reinforced Langet (10 points): This weapon cannot be disarmed.
    • Bearded Extension (20 points): Wild Swing now ignores guard, but the damage is reduced to 2 (light attack) or 4 (heavy attack).
    • Rotational Stability (35 points): If a target moves away from you when you attack with this weapon and your next attack this turn is a throwing attack using this weapon against that target, that attack gains lock on.
  • B mods:
    • Boomerang Wing (25 points): After using a throwing attack with this weapon, you can return the weapon to your hand for 0 RP.
    • Lancebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Lancebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding polearms or lances.

descriptionHtml:

    • Reinforced Langet (10 points): This weapon cannot be disarmed.
    • Bearded Extension (20 points): Wild Swing now ignores guard, but the damage is reduced to 2 (light attack) or 4 (heavy attack).
    • Rotational Stability (35 points): If a target moves away from you when you attack with this weapon and your next attack this turn is a throwing attack using this weapon against that target, that attack gains lock on.
  • B mods:
    • Boomerang Wing (25 points): After using a throwing attack with this weapon, you can return the weapon to your hand for 0 RP.
    • Lancebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Lancebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding polearms or lances.
descriptionText:
Pickaxes are used by Miners in their activities. With proficiency, they are treated as a Two-Handed axe that can be used as a miner's tool.

Mods

- A mods:- Reinforced Langet (10 points): This weapon cannot be disarmed.
- Bearded Extension (20 points): Wild Swing now ignores guard, but the damage is reduced to 2 (light attack) or 4 (heavy attack).
- Rotational Stability (35 points): If a target moves away from you when you attack with this weapon and your next attack this turn is a throwing attack using this weapon against that target, that attack gains lock on.

- B mods:- Boomerang Wing (25 points): After using a throwing attack with this weapon, you can return the weapon to your hand for 0 RP.
- Lancebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Lancebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding polearms or lances.

name: Pistol (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Two-Handed Grip: You may sheath or draw this weapon for 0 AP once per turn. When you use two hands to make attacks with this weapon, you get a +1 (Light or Heavy attack) or +2 (Precise attack) bonus to your accuracy roll.

Range: 60ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Blast Diverter (10 points): Once per turn, attacks from this weapon cause the target to only be able to make heavy attacks of opportunity  against you until the end of your turn.
    • Double Action (35 points): Accuracy is reduced by 3, but attacks with this weapon reduce the target’s ranged accuracy by 1 (stacking) until the start of your next turn regardless of if they hit or not.  Doesn’t affect boss enemies.
  • B mods:
    • Match Barrel (50 points): The range of this weapon is increased by 60ft.
    • Beavertail Grip (50 points): When making a light attack at a target you can move 10ft in a straight line towards them.

Range: 60ft

Mods

- A mods:- Blast Diverter (10 points): Once per turn, attacks from this weapon cause the target to only be able to make heavy attacks of opportunity  against you until the end of your turn.

- B mods:- Match Barrel (50 points): The range of this weapon is increased by 60ft.
- Beavertail Grip (50 points): When making a light attack at a target you can move 10ft in a straight line towards them.

name: Polaris Pair
descriptionHtml:
A pair of blue shortswords that are always calling out to each other.

While the wielder has one of the weapons in their hand, they may summon the other weapon at no cost, regardless of distance. Both swords are treated as Light Swords.

If the dual wield offhand attack made with this weapon is made against a target that was previously attacked by the main hand attack, the attack gains Lock-on.

name: Polearm (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Polearm: You gain +5ft range on your weapon attacks and your threatened range becomes 5-10ft.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Heavy Broadhead (10 points): Increases thrown power by 2 but boomerang/returning effects no longer work.
    • Shepherd's Lanza (20 points): Fall damage is reduced by 60ft while wielding.
    • Halberd (35 points): This weapon also gains the axe passive (Wild Swing).
  • B mods:
    • Winged Lugs (25 points): Increase guard by 1 and Block by 2 and reduce evasion by 1 while wielding.
    • Fullpike (25 points): Polearm weapon passive changes to +10ft and your threatened range becomes 5-15 ft. Having this item in your burden reduces your max RP by 1.
    • Yari Point Taper (50 points): After making a basic heavy attack, strike another target immediately behind the first with a light attack.
    • Swordbreaker (80 points):  You gain a +2 Swordbreaker Power bonus against targets wielding light blades and heavy blades.

name: Portable Vacuum
descriptionHtml:
A large box with a powerful vacuum on top. When activated, it sucks in any mist or gas within 20ft of its location, removing its effects. This affects magical mists or gasses too, but cannot affect those created by Secret Art or Climax Art.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Powerful Fan (10 points): Increases the range of which it sucks in by 10ft.
    • Fuel Optimization (10 points): When activated, it now lasts until the end of the encounter on a single shell use.
  • B mods:
    • Airflow Optimization (25 points): Increases the range of which it sucks in by 10ft.
    • Backpack Vacuum (80 points): You may carry this artifice on your back and you can activate it without having to draw it. In addition, it moves with you instead of sucking up things only near its activation location.

name: Portable Wall
descriptionHtml:
A handful of rock-like objects that when thrown generate a magical wall between each of the rocks. If there is a living creature between the rocks, the wall will not appear. The wall is 10ft high, 5ft long, lasts for 2 turns and has 100 HP. After a single use, this item becomes unusable. Can be thrown up to 40ft away.

Crafting:
Materials: 600 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Earth
  • 1x Mystic Arcane

name: Power Gauntlets
descriptionHtml:
A large and heavy pair of gloves. Once the wearer inserts their hand into them, they will secure themselves properly around their hands. 

When activated, the wearer’s unarmed attacks do normal weapon damage instead of their normal unarmed damage. If the wearer already has a feature that lets them do this, the gauntlets instead give them +1 Power to unarmed attacks. While wearing these gauntlets, even if they are inactive, the wearer suffers a -10 penalty to all checks that require finesse movements with their hands. 

Wearing these gauntlets also prevents the user from properly holding and using other items and weapons with them. Both hands are taken up by these gauntlets.

While they are considered a two-handed weapon as they take two hands to use, they do not normally get 5d6 heavy damage.

After drawing these in combat, putting them on takes 2 AP (But you may choose to just always wear them to bypass this). Taking them off also takes 2 AP. 

Mods:

  • A mods:
    • Fine Size (10 points): Removes the -10 penalty for finesse movements while wearing the gauntlets.
    • Vault Breaker (20 points): Your attacks deal double damage against objects.
  • B mods:
    • Finesse Grip (25 points): You may hold and use items or weapons like normal while wearing these gauntlets.
    • Heavy punches (50 points): Unarmed attacks made with this weapon deal 5d6 damage instead of 4d6.

name: Private Communications Device
descriptionHtml:
A small box that comes with two earplugs. It can connect to other devices within a mile by inputting a code unique to the box its connecting to. When a person speaks into the box, their voice can be heard through the earplugs as normal volume, regardless of how loud the person speaks. Only a person wearing at least 1 earplugs can hear sounds coming through it.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Extra Earplugs (10 points): The device now comes with 3 additional earplugs, letting more people listen in.
  • B mods:
    • Extra Range (25 points): The range of the device increases from 1 mile to 3 miles.
    • Hidden Device (80 points): The device is disguises as a regular object, making it not appear as a private communications device. (Closer inspection or perception checks may still reveal it as such).

name: Projected Recorder Orb
descriptionHtml: A special crystal ball that records sounds and images.  It can be used to playback what it has recorded with illusionary magic.  The shell is built-in and the device records up to 1 minute in a 10ft radius around the orb.  When used again it will project an illusion of the recording around itself.  The illusion appears slightly distorted and is clearly an illusion.  The orb can only record once, but may playback for up to a year.

name: Rabbit Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that increases metabolism when used, increasing speed. When drank, you take 11 true damage.  Until the end of the encounter your speed increases by 10 ft.

Crafting:
Materials: 490 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Wind

An elixir that increases metabolism when used, increasing speed. When drank, you take 11 true damage.  Until the end of the encounter your speed increases by 10 ft.

Crafting:
Materials: 490 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

- 1x Common Wind

name: Reaper Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that causes a surge of energy within the body. When drank, you take 11 true damage. You gain +1 Power. Lasts until the end of combat.

Crafting:
Materials: 350 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Fire
  • 1x Common Arcane

- 1x Common Arcane

name: Refractor Gun
descriptionHtml:
An artifice gun with cartridges that lets it fire blasts of arcane energy through a series of lenses rather than bullets. Attacks with this weapon deal Arcane damage. Exists in Musket, Pistol, Shotgun and Sniper Rifle variants.

Mods:

Also takes normal pistol, shotgun, musket and sniper rifle mods.

  • A mods:
    • Elemental Lens (10 points): Instead of Arcane, the weapon may deal a different element of your choosing
  • B mods:
    • None

name: Research Kit
descriptionHtml:
Has enough bottles/vials/containers to store research samples for one expedition.

The kit is consumed at the end of the expedition if used.
descriptionText:
Has enough bottles/vials/containers to store research samples for one expedition.

The kit is consumed at the end of the expedition if used.

name: Return Stone
descriptionHtml:
A small crystal that is attuned to normal reality. Can be used as an emergency escape from dungeons.

When activated, the person who activated it is immediately teleported out of the dungeon. May be used on downed allies instead of oneselves.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Shortened Activator (20 points): Reduces the activation cost by 1 AP.
  • B mods:
    • Ranged Circuit (25 points): Increases the range by 20ft when used on a downed ally.
    • Re-usable (50 points): Can be used more than once, but requires repair in between them. This also takes up 2 C slots. 
    • Group Escape (80 points): When used, up to 3 other allies within 10ft of you may choose to escape with you. Increases the AP cost by 2, potentially giving it the setup keyword.
    • Re-usable+ (80 points): Can be used more than once. This also takes up 3 C slots.

name: Saboteur Thread Daggers (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Wired Weapon: You may draw or sheath this weapon for 0 AP once per turn. You may throw this weapon up to 30ft as if it was a throwing weapon. You may recall the weapon after throwing it for 0 AP.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Compact Ergonomics (20 points): When your other hand is free and you take a movement action that costs RP, that movement range is increased by 5ft. 
    • Parrying Dagger (20 points): If you don’t use this weapon to make an attack during your turn, you gain a +4 Shield bonus to your Block until the start of your next turn. You also gain a +4 bonus to your ‘damage’ roll when using the Parry ability.
    • Flat Pommel (35 points): When your other hand is free, you use your free hand to push the target into targets increasing melee heavy attack damage to 5d6.
  • B mods:
    • Poisoner Weapon (25 points): Applying poison to this weapon costs -1 AP (min 0).
    • Butterfly Knife (50 points): Your first light attack each round with this weapon gains Trick Attack, but it can no longer make Heavy attacks.
    • Ring Pommel (50 points): If you make a heavy attack that the target defends against, you do not trigger attacks of opportunity from them until the start of your next turn.
    • Rappel (80 points): After you deal damage with a thrown attack with this weapon, you may move up to 30ft towards the target. Can only be done once per round.

name: Scythe (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Sweeping Arc: Once per round, when you make a Heavy attack against a target, you may make a Light attack against a different enemy within your range.

Mods:

  • A mods:
    • Spinning Dance (20 points): You may move 5ft after making your Heavy attack, but before making your Sweeping Arc attack.
    • Long Haft (35 points): You gain +5ft range on your weapon attacks and your threatened range becomes 10ft.
  • B mods:
    • Rapid Spins (50 points): Your Sweeping Arc can now be used two times per round.
    • Focused Spin (50 points): Your Sweeping Arc now instead targets the same creature as the target of your Heavy attack.
    • Full Spin (80 points): Your sweeping arc can now target all creatures within 5ft of you.

- Focused Spin (50 points): Your Sweeping Arc now instead targets the same creature as the target of your Heavy attack.
- Full Spin (80 points): Your sweeping arc can now target all creatures within 5ft of you.

name: Set of Missiles (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Stealthy Throwing: You draw one instance of this weapon at the start of making an attack (the rest remain on your equipment). Making an attack while stealthed with this weapon does not break your stealth.

Range: 30ft

Mods:

  • A mods:
    • Featherflight (35 points): Range is increased to 60ft, but the remain stealthed mechanic only applies when attacking within 30ft.
  • B mods:
    • Poison Container (25 points): Poison can be pre-applied to the container and then sealed. Once the lid is opened and a missile is drawn, it is considered to be applied and begins to use up its application limit and duration.

name: Shark Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that makes temporary physical changes to allow you to breathe underwater. When drank you take 11 true damage. You can breathe underwater and swim easily underwater up to your speed for up to 1 day.

Crafting:
Materials: 350 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Water

An elixir that makes temporary physical changes to allow you to breathe underwater. When drank you take 11 true damage. You can breathe underwater and swim easily underwater up to your speed for up to 1 day.

Crafting:
Materials: 350 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

- 1x Common Water

name: Shell Converter
descriptionHtml:
An artifice that easily converts fuel into shells. Less bulky to carry than an artifice kit, but cannot be used to repair artifices.

Treated as a kit for Burden purposes.

Rest Action: You can choose to create a small Artifice Shell, paying its cost in Magical Fuel to create.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Medium Capacity (20 points): You may also create Medium Shells (costs more Fuel still).
  • B mods:
    • Increased Production (25 points): You may create two shells instead of 1.
    • Lightweight Design (80 points): This artifice is no longer counted as a kit and can thus be stored easily in non-combat inventory.

name: Shepherd's Sling (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
A sling that throws carved stones at high speed as a projectile.  

Range: 60ft

Giant Killer:  Cannot be dual wielded with another sling. You can overcharge your basic heavy attack for 1 AP to deal 9 extra damage.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Soon
  • B mods:
    • Anti-Goliath (50 points): You gain a +1 Size Power bonus on attacks against creatures that are Large or bigger.
    • Explosive Rock (80 points): When making the Overcharge attack, you may deal the extra damage to all creatures within 5ft of the original target. In addition, this extra damage becomes a Light damage roll for all creatures except the original target instead of the flat 9 damage.

name: Shield
descriptionHtml:
Gives a +4 Shield bonus to your Block.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Spiked Shield (20 points): You may treat your shield as a One-Handed weapon. You can use it to make an attack and still gain the shield bonus.
    • Mirror Shield (35 points): When you are the target of a Mystic Eye ability, you may spend 1 RP to negate the effect on yourself and reflect it back to the user.
  • B mods:
    • Throwing Shield (50 points): You may throw this shield at an enemy within 30ft to make a ranged attack against it. Afterwards, the shield returns to your hand.
    • Shield Formation (80 points): While next to another ally with a shield or greatshield, you gain +1 Guard. (Only actual shields apply)
descriptionText:
Gives a +4 Shield bonus to your Block.

name: Shield (Great)
descriptionHtml:
Gives a +1 Shield bonus to your guard, but a -1 penalty to your Initiative and a -2 penalty to your Evasion and Dodge. When taking the Block action, this Shield bonus is increased to 8.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Spiked Shield (20 points): You may treat your shield as a One-Handed weapon. You can use it to make an attack and still gain the shield bonus.
    • Mirror Shield (35 points): When you are the target of a Mystic Eye ability, you may spend 1 RP to negate the effect on yourself and reflect it back to the user.
  • B mods:
    • Tower Shield (50 points): The burden of this shield is increased by 1, but it now counts as Low cover for you against ranged attacks.
    • Shield Formation (80 points): While next to another ally with a shield or greatshield, you gain +1 Guard. (Only actual shields apply)
descriptionText:
Gives a +1 Shield bonus to your guard, but a -1 penalty to your Initiative and a -2 penalty to your Evasion and Dodge. When taking the Block action, this Shield bonus is increased to 8.

Mods

- A mods:- Spiked Shield (20 points): You may treat your shield as a One-Handed weapon. You can use it to make an attack and still gain the shield bonus.
- Mirror Shield (35 points): When you are the target of a Mystic Eye ability, you may spend 1 RP to negate the effect on yourself and reflect it back to the user.

- B mods:- Tower Shield (50 points): The burden of this shield is increased by 1, but it now counts as Low cover for you against ranged attacks.
- Shield Formation (80 points): While next to another ally with a shield or greatshield, you gain +1 Guard. (Only actual shields apply)

name: Shielding Potion
descriptionHtml:
A potion that briefly forms a protective barrier around you when drunk. You gain 15 temporary hit points that last until the start of your next turn.

Crafting:
Materials: 270 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 3x Common Earth

Herbs:

name: Shock Stick
descriptionHtml:
A long stick that can easily be used by anyone proficient in any melee weapon. 

Considered an improvised weapon until it is activated, after which it is considered a light weapon that does normal Lightning damage. Upon reducing an enemy to 0 or lower HP, the target becomes stunned instead of killed. This stun lasts until the end of the encounter and an extra minute after that.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Heavy Baton (20 points): This weapon is now considered a two-handed weapon.
    • Long Stick (35 points): You gain +5ft range on your weapon attacks and your threatened range becomes 10ft
  • B mods:
    • Capture Model (80 points): When dealing damage to a Grunt with a Heavy attack, they are stunned until  the start of your next turn even if they are still above 0 HP.

name: Short Range Communications Device
descriptionHtml:
Two small boxes that are connected to each other. When a button is pressed and both are active, all audio from one of the boxes is transmitted to the other one. It has a maximum range of 1 mile.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Directional Ping (35 points): While within the device's range, you can determine the direction the paired device is in.
  • B mods:
    • Extra Range (25 points): The range of the device increases from 1 mile to 2 miles.

name: Shotgun (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Point Blank: Can be fired while in melee range of an enemy without provoking an attack of opportunity.

Range: 15ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Dragon's Breath (10 points): The first basic attack you make with this weapon in a round inflicts burning 5 regardless of it hits or not. (If used with Open Choke mod, this can affect that attack).
    • Proof Load (20 points): When you make a heavy attack with this weapon, you may knock yourself back 5ft.
    • Point Blank (20 points): You may make AoO’s as if this was a melee weapon with a 5ft threaten range.
  • B mods:
    • Slugs (50 points): Your range is doubled.
    • Open Choke (80 points): The first basic light attack you make with this weapon in a round makes a Light attack against all enemies in a 45 degree cone up to range.

name: Sickle
descriptionHtml:
Sickle's are used by Farmers in their activities. With proficiency, they are treated as a One-Handed axe that can be used as a farmer's tool.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Reinforced Langet (10 points): This weapon cannot be disarmed.
    • Bearded Extension (20 points): Wild Swing now ignores guard, but the damage is reduced to 2 (light attack) or 4 (heavy attack).
    • Rotational Stability (35 points): If a target moves away from you when you attack with this weapon and your next attack this turn is a throwing attack using this weapon against that target, that attack gains lock on.
  • B mods:
    • Boomerang Wing (25 points): After using a throwing attack with this weapon, you can return the weapon to your hand for 0 RP.
    • Lancebreaker (80 points): You gain a +2 Lancebreaker Power bonus against enemies wielding polearms or lances.

name: Silencer
descriptionHtml:
A tool for guns that reduces the sound they make. Can be applied to weapons of the Musket, Pistol, Shotgun and Sniper Rifle weapon groups. When applied, these weapons no longer make a very loud noise when fired.

When a gun is fired without a silencer, the loud noise will alert all enemies within 120ft of the shooter.

Can be attached or detached from a weapon by spending 4 AP.

A tool for guns that reduces the sound they make. Can be applied to weapons of the Musket, Pistol, Shotgun and Sniper Rifle weapon groups. When applied, these weapons no longer make a very loud noise when fired.

When a gun is fired without a silencer, the loud noise will alert all enemies within 120ft of the shooter.

Can be attached or detached from a weapon by spending 4 AP.

name: Small Shell
descriptionHtml: A small cylinder of magical fuel that is used to power artifices.  Most weapon-type artifices automatically eject the shell when they are emptied… along with a burst of steam to prevent overheating.
descriptionText: A small cylinder of magical fuel that is used to power artifices.  Most weapon-type artifices automatically eject the shell when they are emptied… along with a burst of steam to prevent overheating.

name: Small Weapons (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Hidden Weapon: You may draw this weapon for 0 AP. Each combat, after you draw the weapon if the next attack you make that round is using this weapon and is a basic Light or Heavy attack, it gains the Trick Attack keyword. After benefiting from this bonus, you cannot do so again the same combat even if drawing a different weapon with this ability.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Aerodynamic Design (10 points): Boomerang/Return effects cannot be used with this. For 1 RP you can throw the dagger, making a light ranged attack.  On damage, the target is slowed until the end of its next turn.
    • Spring-loaded (20 points): You do not need to draw and wield this weapon to use it, it is worn in your equipment. However, you can only attack once per round with it. If your first attack with it in a fight is a basic attack, it activates Hidden Weapon as if you drew it right before attacking, though this still does not allow you to benefit from Hidden Weapon multiple times per combat. This weapon can no longer be used to make thrown weapon attacks and cannot be drawn normally. Designed by Nya.
    • Compact Ergonomics (20 points): When your other hand is free and you take a movement action that costs RP, that movement range is increased by 5ft. 
    • Parrying Dagger (20 points): If you don’t use this weapon to make an attack during your turn, you gain a +4 Shield bonus to your Block until the start of your next turn. You also gain a +4 bonus to your ‘damage’ roll when using the Parry ability.
    • Flat Pommel (35 points): When your other hand is free, you use your free hand to push the blade into targets increasing melee heavy attack damage to 5d6.
  • B mods:
    • Poisoner Weapon (25 points): Applying poison to this weapon costs -1 AP (min 0).
    • Butterfly Knife (50 points): Your first light attack each round with this weapon gains Trick Attack, but it can no longer make Heavy attacks.
    • Ring Pommel (50 points): If you make a heavy attack that the target defends against, you do not trigger attacks of opportunity from them until the start of your next turn.

name: Smith's Hammer (One-handed)
descriptionHtml:
Core crafting tool. Allows you to craft items as a Blacksmith or Armorsmith.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Flexible Handle (10 points): When dealing damage to a target, the minimum damage you deal is always 2. (this does not trigger on damage effects if damage would be 0 otherwise).
    • Chain Flail (35 points): Cannot be defended against by 0 RP actions. Attacks made with this weapon do not count as consuming 0 RP actions.
  • B mods:
    • Weighted Poll Upsetting (25 points): When making 3 separate attacks against a target, the third attack you make with Weighted Poll Upsetting knocks the target back 10ft on damage.
    • Flanged Head (50 points): When you crit with this weapon, the damage is still maximized even against blocking targets.

name: Smoke Flask
descriptionHtml:
An Alchemical mix that creates a cloud of smoke temporarily. May be thrown up to 20ft away. Creates a smokecloud in a 15x15ft area that blocks line of sight. This effect functions similarly to ‘Glittershards’ and ends at the start of your next turn.

Crafting:
Materials: 200 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Wind

name: Sniper Rifle (Two-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Snipe: Looking through a sniper scope gives you a +10 bonus to Perception when looking at things further than 60ft away.

Range: 720ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Semi-Automatic (20 points): The first time you miss with a basic Heavy attack in a round you can follow up with a light attack against the same target. This counts as your dual wield attack.
    • Hollow Point Boat Tail Rounds (35 points): You gain a +1 bonus to your accuracy when making a basic Heavy or precise attack.
    • Armor Piercing Rounds (35 points): The first attack you make each round with this weapon treats the enemy guard as if it were 4 lower.
  • B mods:
    • Bipod (50 points): All attacks with this weapon gain the Prone keyword. While prone, you are considered to have low cover against ranged attacks.
    • Snipe (80 points): If you did not move this turn or the previous turn you gain a +1d4 bonus to your accuracy roll on attacks made with a Sniper Rifle. If you already have the Sniper's Snipe ability, the effect is 4 instead of rolling when used with this weapon.

name: Snow Cloak
descriptionHtml:
A white cloak designed as the counterpart to the Fire Robe. Keeps the wearer warm in even the coldest of weathers.

Can be activated to give the wearer 5 damage reduction against frost damage until the end of the encounter. When this effect ends, the shell inside the cloak is immediately used up and needs to be replaced for the weather repelling effect to function.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced to 0 AP.
  • B mods:
    • Snowproof (25 points): While it's active, you are immune to Slowing effects caused by Frost damage.
    • Increased Protection (80 points): The damage reduction is increased to 10.

- B mods:- Snowproof (25 points): While it's active, you are immune to Slowing effects caused by Frost damage.

name: Soul Lamp
descriptionHtml:
A lamp that can be attuned to the soul of a single person. While that person is alive, the lamp emits a light glow. Should the person die, the lamp’s light fades and the lamp then shatters into pieces.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Vivre Flame(10 points): The person who is attuned to the lamp must be present upon its creation, and a bit of their blood or magic must be inserted into the lamp. The soul lamp’s flame’s strength now corresponds to the health of the target, being a bright flame when the target is at 100% HP and becoming a Dim flicker when they are downed.
  • B mods:
    • Damage Sensor (25 points): When the target takes damage, the flame flickers a different color.
    • Directional Flame (80 points): The lamp contains a flame that points in the direction of the person the lamp is attuned to.

name: Souleater Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that heightens spirit circuits in the eyes, making the user able to view invisible creatures. When drank you take 11 true damage.  You gain the ability to see invisible (not hidden) creatures and gain a +10 bonus to Perception checks to detect creatures until the end of the encounter.

Crafting:
Materials: 1100 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Fire
  • 1x Common Water

name: Space Time Anchor
descriptionHtml:
A large box that when put down and activated projects an area in a 40ft radius around it. Any creature inside cannot use any teleportation abilities. Any creature outside of the area trying to teleport inside is placed at the nearest edge of the area.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quickened Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced by 1 AP.
  • B mods:
    • Increased Range (25 points): Increases the radius of the device by 10ft.
    • Anchor Backpack (50 points): You may carry the device around instead of placing it on the ground. However, your movement speed is reduced by 10ft if doing so while the device is active.
    • Make Exception (80 points): Allies can still teleport while in the area.

name: Speed Potion
descriptionHtml:
A potion that briefly increases your movement speed when drunk. Your movement speed is increased by 20ft until the end of your turn. 

Crafting:
Materials: 270 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 3x Common Wind

name: Spider Shoes
descriptionHtml:
A pair of shoes that can be adjusted to fit the wearer. Upon use, the wearer is able to walk across any angled surface as if it was normal solid ground

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Web Resistance (10 points): You don't suffer any penalties from walking across webs either natural or created through magic or items.
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced by 1 AP, to a minimum of 0.
    • Ceiling Drop (20 points): You may cancel the effect of this artifice for 0 RP. When you do, if you were attached to a ceiling or vertical surface, treat the fall as if it was 100ft lower than it is.
  • B mods:
    • Sticky Footing (50 points): You gain a +5 bonus to any saves that would knock you prone or knock you back.
    • Skitter Away (80 points): When you move up a vertical surface from a horizontal one, your first movement of the round does not provoke an attack of opportunity.

name: Staff (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
On damage with an attack made with this weapon, you may spend 1 Mana to push the target back 5ft.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Elemental Staff (35 points): You may alter the damage type from an attack made with this weapon for 0 AP. The damage type must be an element type you have mastered (usually unlocked through classes) or one that’s built into the weapon.
    • Powerful Push (35 points): The knockback distance is increased to 10ft.
  • B mods:
    • Efficiency (50 points): The first basic attack you make each round may trigger the knockback effect without spending mana.
    • Unstoppable Force (80 points): The knockback effect is now on hit rather than on damage.

name: Sticky Glue Flask
descriptionHtml:
An Alchemical mix that coats the target in Sticky Glue, restricting mobility. May be thrown up to 40ft away. When thrown, the target of the flask takes 1 damage. If they took damage, their evasion is reduced by 3 until they spend 2 AP to wipe the substance off.

Crafting:
Materials: 500 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Earth

An Alchemical mix that coats the target in Sticky Glue, restricting mobility. May be thrown up to 40ft away. When thrown, the target of the flask takes 1 damage. If they took damage, their evasion is reduced by 3 until they spend 2 AP to wipe the substance off.

Crafting:
Materials: 500 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

- 1x Rare Earth

name: Stone Serpent's Kiss
descriptionHtml:
By Boshi

Contact poison(Venom). Can be applied to a cutting or thrusting weapon- or to an arrow, bolt, or bullet. Lasts for 1 hit or 1 minute (6 rounds). 

When an attack with this poison damages an enemy, they are inflicted with a form of Paralysis. A paralyzed Heroic or Boss target is Rooted and Unbalanced until recovering from this poison, which lasts for 1 minute (6 rounds). A paralyzed grunt is completely incapacitated (0AP, 0RP) until they recover from this poison, which lasts for 1 hour (360 rounds). 

A Heroic or Boss unit afflicted may spend 2 AP or make a Save at the end of their turn for free against Potency 13 to end the effect early on each of their turns.

Crafting:
Materials: 900 Alchemy Materials
Special Materials: 

  • 1 Rare Wind Herb 
  • 1 Rare Arcane Herb 
  • 1 Mystic Earth Herb

name: Stun Gun
descriptionHtml:
A Light gun that can be used by anyone proficient in Muskets, Pistols, Shotguns or Sniper Rifles. It has a range of 60ft and does normal Lightning damage. Upon reducing an enemy to 0 or lower HP, the target becomes stunned instead of killed. This stun lasts until the end of the encounter and an extra minute after that.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Training Stock (10 points): You can use this weapon even if you're not proficient with any of the above listed firearms.
    • Long Range (35 points): The range of the weapon is increased to 120ft.
  • B mods:
    • Capture Model (80 points): When dealing damage to a Grunt with a Heavy attack, they are stunned until  the start of your next turn even if they are still above 0 HP.

name: Suppression Potion
descriptionHtml:
A potion that temporarily suppresses the effects of a poison, disease or other natural affliction. The effect lasts for 24 hours. Magical poisons, diseases, curses etc are not affected by this.

Crafting:
Materials: 600 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Rare Water
  • 1x Rare Earth
descriptionText:

name: Surgery Kit
descriptionHtml:
Surgery kits contain stitches, bandages and other items used for more serious wounds.

Rest Action: You perform minor surgery on an ally or yourself. Requires at least 5 points in Medicine (or a relevant expertise in Medicine) to use. Make a medicine check against DC 15.  On success you can negate the effect of one injury suffered this campaign until the end of the campaign.  An injury can only be negated once by this effect, and one person can only have one negated injury at a time.  The injury must still be dealt with after the campaign ends.

name: Thrown (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Switch Hit: This weapon can be used as a melee weapon without penalty. After making a ranged attack with this weapon, it returns to your hand.

Range: 30ft

Mods:

  • A mods:
    • Skirmisher (35 points): After making a ranged attack with this weapon, you may move 5ft as if you had the light sword passive (Nimble).
    • Spinning Axe (35 Points): This weapon also gains the axe passive (Wild Swing). You keep this effect even when throwing the weapon.
  • B mods:
    • Throwing Hammer (50 points): Attacks with this weapon gain the Artillery keyword within 30ft.
    • Ricocheting (50 points): Once per round when this weapon deals damage with a Heavy attack, you may make a Light attack against a different target within 20ft.

name: Timeworn Katana
descriptionHtml:
Current Wielder: Unknown

A rusty katana with a dull blade.

 

Any attack made with this weapon does not deal damage immediately, but any damage it deals is stored (after the hit resolves). When the wielder sheathes this weapon, it deals true damage based on the damage stored. The damage each target takes is based on the damage that was stored against them.

Hits and damage remain stored this way for up to the end of an encounter or for up to a minute, whichever is longer.

name: Torch
descriptionHtml: A simple piece of wood with a pre-oiled tied cloth on one end.  Provides ample light and is quite easy to light on fire.  Comes with an attached striker that can be lit on most rock and metal surfaces. When ‘activated’, creates a light that emits light within 30 feet.  Can also be used as an improvised weapon that deals fire damage.  Lasts up to two hours.

Comes with an attached striker that can be lit on most rock and metal surfaces. When ‘activated’, creates a light that emits light within 30 feet.  Can also be used as an improvised weapon that deals fire damage.  Lasts up to two hours.

name: Transforming Weapon
descriptionHtml:
A transforming weapon is a weapon attachment that can attach to both melee and ranged weapons. While attached to two weapons, the wielder can press a button to consume a shell and transform the current active weapon into the other attached weapon.

Up to 5 shells fit into one weapon.

Weapons can be attached or detached for 4 AP each.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Smooth Operations (20 points): The first switch in an encounter does not consume a shell.
  • B mods:
    • Triple Form (50 points): You may attach 3 weapons to this instead of 2.
    • Quick Swap (80 points): You may activate this artifice for 0 RP instead of 0 AP.

name: Tunnel Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that causes intense focus on the task at hand. When drank you take 11 true damage. You gain +1 Focus. Lasts until the end of combat.

Crafting:
Materials: 380 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 2x Common Earth

An elixir that gives you greater endurance. When drank, you take 11 true damage. You gain +1 Toughness. Lasts until the end of combat.

name: Twinblade (Two-handed)
descriptionHtml:
Twin Strike: You can make dual wield attacks as if you were wielding two weapons.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Haladie Blade (20 points): Attacks with this weapon have -1 Power, but you can make 2 offhand attacks per turn instead of one.
  • B mods:
    • Shadow-Type Saber (80 points): When engaged in melee and you take a block or dodge action and then you are attacked by another melee attack by a different enemy, you may use block or dodge against that attack for 0 RP.  Applies once per turn

name: Universal Armor Mods
descriptionHtml:
Base Mods:

These are mods that can be put on any armor (not shield), regardless of type. The crafting cost to add these is listed after the name.

A mods:

  • Ceremonial Armor (10 points): You gain +10 bonus to negotiation and intimidation checks, but automatically fail any stealth checks.
  • Cast Off Design (10 points): After using the Block action, you may spend 0 RP to cast off the armor immediately unequipping it. The armor becomes Broken until repaired.
  • Hidden Armor (10 points): This armor looks like normal clothing to other creatures (which may make you look more vulnerable than you are).
  • Ki Concealment (20 points): Requires Manaweave Frame mod. While wearing this armor you gain presence concealment. Spending any mana immediately removes presence concealment. After losing presence concealment, you can’t regain it until you rest for the day.

B mods:

  • Mana Barrier (25 points): Requires Manaweave Frame mod. While putting on this armor, your mana and max mana is set to 0. For each mana you lose thanks to this effect, you gain 7 temporary HP. When you would regain mana or HP from resting, this temporary HP regenerates (up to a max of what you started with at the start of the arc)
  • Sensory Suit (50 points): This armor boosts the range of Mind Connection and Scry by 10x.  Also reduces the mana cost of Psychometry by 1.

Frame mods:

  • Barrier (20 points): This armor provides no passive guard bonus, but reduces the Initiative penalty by 2 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 4. When taking the Block action, you still receive the normal bonus the armor would provide.
  • Protective Coating (20 points): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but only gives the passive guard increase. When taking the Block action, it does not gain an increased guard (though any other bonuses to your Block such as your Toughness still apply).
  • Manaweave (20 points): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2. but reduces the wearer's current and maximum mana by 3 when equipped. Cannot be equipped if it would reduce the mana below 0.

Flamellon Mods:

Flamellon mods are special mods that can be put on armors. To put one of these mods on an item you must have sufficient items with the matching Flamelic Polarity.

Adding a layering of the required element automatically fulfills gives polarity equal to the units in the ingot. For example, adding a Salachar ingot as layering also gives you 3000 Salachar polarity.

  • Arcanium
    • Quick Channel Layer (B mod, 40 points, 2000 Arcanium Polarity): Your maximum mana is increased by 1 while wearing this armor.
  • Salachar
    • Fire Armor (Frame mod, 20 points, 600 Salachar Polarity): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but you take double damage from Water damage.
    • Refractory Plates (A mod, 35 points, 1000 Salachar Polarity): You become immune to Burning and natural heat effects.
    • Sunfire Infusion (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Salachar Polarity): All creatures ending their turn next to you take Light Fire damage.
  • Sylphsteel
    • Turbulent Air Layer (Frame mod, 20 points, 600 Sylphsteel Polarity): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but the bonuses of this armor only apply against ranged attacks.
    • Wind's Blessing (B mod, 25 points, 1250 Sylphsteel Polarity): After you Dodge, you gain +10ft of movement speed until the end of your next turn.
    • Cloud Pillow (B mod, 25 points 1250 Sylphsteel Polarity): When you would take falling or pushback damage, you treat the distance you would fall as 30ft lower.
    • Aerolevitation Effect (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Sylphsteel Polarity): You may Hover.
  • Fulgarite
    • Lightning Armor (Frame mod, 20 points, 600 Fulgarite Polarity): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but you take double damage from Earth damage.
    • Static Armor (B mod, 25 points, 1250 Fulgarite Polarity): Anyone ending their turn next to you is afflicted with Static 12 that triggers at the end of their next turn.
    • Franklin Badge (B mod, 50 points, 2500 Fulgarite Polarity): Any attack dealing Lightning damage that targets an ally within 30ft of you and doesn’t include you as a target suffers a -5 penalty to Accuracy.
    • Lightning Reprisal (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Fulgarite Polarity): The first time each round you take damage from a Heavy attack in melee, you may make a Light attack against the attacker.
  • Corvuskite
    • Shadow Buoyancy (Frame mod, 20 points, 600 Corvuskite Polarity): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2 during the night.
    • Light Absorbing Layer (A mod, 10 points, 300 Corvuskite Polarity): You gain +5 stealth during the night (doesn’t stack with cloak).
  • Vivinthal
    • Condensation Layer (Frame mod, 20 points, 600 Vivinthal Polarity): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but you take double damage from Lightning damage and you are considered permanently soaked.
    • Bubble Flow Armor (B mod, 25 points, 1250 Vivinthal Polarity): You gain a swim speed of 20ft and can hold your breath for twice as long.
    • Galilean Latticing (B mod, 25 points, 1250 Vivinthal Polarity): You may walk on water as if it was normal ground.
    • Fog Plates (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Vivinthal Polarity): You gain a +4 bonus to your Dodge.
  • Glacite
    • Ice Armor (Frame Mod, 20 points, 600 Glacite Polarity): Reduces the Initiative penalty by 1 and the Evasion/Dodge penalty by 2, but you take double damage from Fire damage.
    • Permafrost Mantle (B mod, 50 points, 2500 Glacite Polarity): Enemies within 5ft of you are slowed. This slow is applied during a movement if they walk within 5ft of you. Lasts until they are no longer within 5ft of you, but it applies for the entire movement if leaving the range.
  • Ghimslag:
    • Seismographic Reflex Layer (B mod, 50 points, 2500 Ghimslag Polarity): You gain 20ft of Tremorsense.
    • Rock Plates (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Ghimslag Polarity): You gain a +8 bonus to your Block.
  • Luxaeterna:
    • Luxflow Coating (A mod 20 points, 600 Luxaeterna Polarity): You radiate a bright light that illuminates up to 60ft away.
    • Triboluminescent Plates (B mod 80 points, 4000 Lux Aeterna Polarity): Undead creatures that deal damage to you take Light Holy damage in return.
  • Aetherplumes (feathers/wings with high magic concentration.  Tengu/Harpy/Fae etc):
    • Tengu Armor (A mod 35 points, 1000 Aetherplume Polarity): Your movement speed increases by 5ft while hovering or flying.
    • Aerodynamic Glider (A mod 35 points, 1000 Aetherplume Polarity): You may spend 1 RP to glide (as per the aeromancer ability)
  • Phoenix Materials (Phoenix Core):
    • Trauma Heat (B mod 80 points, 4000 Phoenix Polarity): Once per round, when you drop to 0 or lower HP while above 0 HP, you may immediately revive at 1 HP. You still gain an injury.
  • Gravity Materials (Gravity Orb):
    • Repulsive Sheeting (A mod 35 points, 1000 Gravity Polarity): When you push or pull a target, you may push or pull them for an additional 5ft.
    • Anti-Terror Field (B mod 50 points, 2500 Gravity Polarity): You gain a +4 bonus to your evasion against ranged attacks, but you cannot make any ranged attacks yourself (they automatically miss).
  • Plant (Ygdrassil Seed):
    • Dryad Bark (A mod 35 points, 1000 Plant Polarity): You can move through natural difficult terrain without losing movement.
    • Resinosis (B mod 50 points, 2500 Plant Polarity): When you take the Block action against an enemy attack, you heal for 3 HP.
  • Silver:
    • Ionic Field (B mod 25 points, 1250 Silver Polarity): Your astra corruption resistance is increased by 1.
  • Insectoid (Fae Cicada Shell):
    • Hive Mind (A mod 35, 1000 Insectoid Polarity): You gain a +1 to initiative for every allied creature (including yourself) wearing an armor with this mod (max +3).
    • Bee Armor (B mod 50 points, 2000 Insectoid Polarity): The first time each combat you take damage from an attack, the attacker becomes Dazed as bees attack it.
    • Living Armor (B mod 80, 4000 Insectoid Polarity): The first time each combat you take damage from a melee attack, you release a swarm of insects in a 5ft radius around you. The swarm remains in the same spot and any creature other than you ending its turn inside of it takes 6 full pierce damage.
  • Undead (Soulstone):
    • Reanimated Bone (B mod 25 points. 1250 Undead Polarity): When you are downed, attacks against you do not gain Full Pierce as your armor actively protects you.
    • Hands of Ptah (B mod 25 points, 1250 Undead Polarity): While you are downed, a necromantic hand activates on your armor, performing emergency medical aid, you regain 2 HP per turn.
    • False Bone Layer (B mod 80 points, 4000 Undead Polarity): Critical hits against you do not gain Full Pierce (but still do max damage)
  • Spidersilk:
    • Slippery (B mod 25 points, 1250 Spidersilk Polarity): You automatically escape from the first grapple effect against you each encounter. 
    • Sticky (B mod 50 points, 2500 Spidersilk Polarity): You gain Spider Climb while wearing this armor (as per spiderfolk racial)
  • Poison Materials (Toxic Core)
    • Poison Inlay (B mod 25 points, 1250 Poison Polarity): You may apply a poison to this armor as a Rest action. When you are hit by a melee attack, the poison explodes outwards and automatically triggers on the attacker as if you had dealt damage to them.
    • Poison Resistance (B mod 50 points, 2500 Poison Polarity): You gain 5 DR against Poison damage.
