- Resinosis (B mod 50 points, 2500 Plant Polarity): When you take the Block action against an enemy attack, you heal for 3 HP.

- Silver:- Ionic Field (B mod 25 points, 1250 Silver Polarity): Your astra corruption resistance is increased by 1.

- Insectoid (Fae Cicada Shell):- Hive Mind (A mod 35, 1000 Insectoid Polarity): You gain a +1 to initiative for every allied creature (including yourself) wearing an armor with this mod (max +3).
- Bee Armor (B mod 50 points, 2000 Insectoid Polarity): The first time each combat you take damage from an attack, the attacker becomes Dazed as bees attack it.
- Living Armor (B mod 80, 4000 Insectoid Polarity): The first time each combat you take damage from a melee attack, you release a swarm of insects in a 5ft radius around you. The swarm remains in the same spot and any creature other than you ending its turn inside of it takes 6 full pierce damage.

- Undead (Soulstone):- Reanimated Bone (B mod 25 points. 1250 Undead Polarity): When you are downed, attacks against you do not gain Full Pierce as your armor actively protects you.
- Hands of Ptah (B mod 25 points, 1250 Undead Polarity): While you are downed, a necromantic hand activates on your armor, performing emergency medical aid, you regain 2 HP per turn.
- False Bone Layer (B mod 80 points, 4000 Undead Polarity): Critical hits against you do not gain Full Pierce (but still do max damage)

- Spidersilk:- Slippery (B mod 25 points, 1250 Spidersilk Polarity): You automatically escape from the first grapple effect against you each encounter.
- Sticky (B mod 50 points, 2500 Spidersilk Polarity): You gain Spider Climb while wearing this armor (as per spiderfolk racial)

- Poison Materials (Toxic Core)- Poison Inlay (B mod 25 points, 1250 Poison Polarity): You may apply a poison to this armor as a Rest action. When you are hit by a melee attack, the poison explodes outwards and automatically triggers on the attacker as if you had dealt damage to them.
- Poison Resistance (B mod 50 points, 2500 Poison Polarity): You gain 5 DR against Poison damage.

name: Universal Artifice Mods
descriptionHtml:
Base Mods:

These are mods that can be put on any artifice, regardless of type. The crafting cost to add these is listed after the name.

A mods:

  •  

B mods:

  •  

C mods:

  • Double Barrel (10 points): The artifice can hold up to 2 shells. You still reload 1 shell at a time.
  • Triple Barrel (10 points): The artifice can hold up to 3 shells. You still reload 1 shell at a time. Requires Double Barrel on the artifice.
  • Quadruple Barrel (10 points): The artifice can hold up to 4 shells. You still reload 1 shell a time. Requires Triple Barrel on the artifice.
  • Quintuple Barrel (10 points): The artifice can hold up to 5 shells. You still reload 1 shell a time. Requires Quadruple Barrel on the artifice.
  • Advanced Melding (10 points): The burden cost of this artifice is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0, if you have at least 1 other artifice with this mod in your combat burden. Only takes effect once, even with 3+ items with this mod. Does not work on artifice weapons that are their own weapon already, such as power gauntlets.
  • Superior Melding (10 points): The burden cost of this artifice is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0, if you have at least 1 other artifice with this mod in your combat burden. Only takes effect once, even with 3+ items with this mod. Does not work on artifice weapons that are their own weapon already, such as power gauntlets. Requires Advanced Melding mod on the artifice.
  • Perfect Melding (10 points): The burden cost of this artifice is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0, if you have at least 1 other artifice with this mod in your combat burden. Only takes effect once, even with 3+ items with this mod. Does not work on artifice weapons that are their own weapon already, such as power gauntlets. Requires Superior Melding mod on the artifice.

name: Universal Crafting Mods
descriptionHtml:
A mods:

  • Cross Peen Hammer (30 points): When crafting two of the same one-handed weapon with the same mods and materials, you may use double materials and only use one crafting attempt to make a pair of weapons.
  • Westrian Style Tool (10 points): You no longer roll dice and instead take 5 for every d10 crafting roll.
  • Sylvan Style Tool (20 points): While crafting points are 30 or less, you gain a +1 bonus to your crafting rolls. 
  • Kiraran Style Tool (30 points): You may reroll any one dice when crafting immediately after determining the result.

B mods:

  • Expertise (80 points): You gain a +1 bonus to crafting checks with a specific armor group, weapon group (or tools) or any other valid expertise.

name: Universal Translator
descriptionHtml:
A small black box. 

When a creature speaks into it while activated, everyone who hears the creature’s voice can automatically understand what they are saying, regardless of the language they are speaking in. 

It cannot translate for creatures that lack a proper language such as animals.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Parley Mode (20 points): The device now translates all words spoken near it, allowing for easier two way communication.
  • B mods:
    • Voice Amplification (50 points): In addition to translating, the speaker's voice is also amplified to be heard from over long distances as if shouting.
descriptionText:
A small black box.

When a creature speaks into it while activated, everyone who hears the creature’s voice can automatically understand what they are saying, regardless of the language they are speaking in.

It cannot translate for creatures that lack a proper language such as animals.

Mods

- A mods:- Parley Mode (20 points): The device now translates all words spoken near it, allowing for easier two way communication.

- B mods:- Voice Amplification (50 points): In addition to translating, the speaker's voice is also amplified to be heard from over long distances as if shouting.

name: Universal Weapon Mods
descriptionHtml:
Base Mods:

These are mods that can be put on any weapon, regardless of type. The crafting cost to add these is listed after the name.

While gathering is a universal mod, you still cannot gather unless the weapon is a tool that can be used for gathering.

A mods:

  • Ninja Weapon (10 points): This weapon is treated as a weapon that can be used for Ninjutsu.
  • Quickdraw (10 points): You may draw this weapon for 0 AP.
  • Gathering (35 points): This weapon gives a +1 bonus to gathering rolls its used in.
  • Perfect Balance (20 points): When using the weapon in dual wielding, you can perform a dual wield attack with your other weapon, even when making a heavy or precise attack with this weapon.
  • Surprise Weapon (35 points): The weapon is designed to look like a mundane object.  Each Combat, after you draw the weapon, if the next attack you make that round is using this weapon and is a basic light or heavy attack it gains the Trick Attack keyword.  After benefitting from this bonus, you cannot do so again the same combat even if drawing a different weapon with with Surprise Weapon or Hidden Weapon.
  • Versatile (10 points): Can be added to a 1h weapon from a group that also has an option to be a 2h weapon (ie Axe, but not small weapons). Increases the burden of the weapon by 1, but the weapon can be used as either a one-handed or two-handed weapon. Must declare which version you are using at the start of your turn, before you making any attacks with this weapon.

B mods:

  • Perfect Edge (25 points): An accuracy roll of 20 gains Sure Hit. If you roll a 20 and choose to have this apply, any Full Pierce is removed from the attack.
  • Momentum (25 points): After downing or killing an enemy, you may Sidestep up to your speed towards another enemy as part of that action.
  • Gathering II (80 points): This weapon gives a +1 bonus to gathering rolls its used in. Stacks with Gathering mod.

Alloy Mods:

Alloy mods can also be put on any weapon, but require the weapon to include a specific type of alloy. If the weapon does not include this alloy, the mod cannot be used.

  • Arcanium
    • Barrier Weaving (A mod, 20 points): When you apply a Shield to an ally or yourself, your shield is treated as if the stat it scales off is 1 higher. (If it doesn’t scale off a stat, there is no effect)
  • Salachar
    • Burning Weapon (B mod, 50 points): The first basic attack made with this weapon each round inflicts Burning 18 to the target on damage until the start of your next turn.
  • Ghimslag:
    • Slowing Weapon (B mod, 50 points): The first basic attack made with this weapon each round slows the target on damage until the start of your next turn.
  • Fulgorite:
    • Thundering Weapon (B mod, 25 points): The first basic attack made with this weapon each round inflicts Static 18 to the target on damage.
    • Conductor (B mod, 50 points):  When this weapon deals Lightning damage against a soaked target, it gains Half Pierce.
  • Luxaeterna:
    • Shining Weapon (A mod, 10 points): Your weapon emits a bright light in a 60ft radius. Can be enabled or disabled for 1 AP.
    • Healer Weapon (A mod, 20 points): When you heal an ally or yourself, you heal as if your power (or focus, agility or toughness if the heal scales off those) was 1 higher.
    • Undead Slayer (B mod, 50 points): When this weapon deals holy damage, it always deals Full Pierce damage against undead unless the undead resists Holy damage.
    • Shielding Healer (B mod, 50 points): When you cast a healing spell that costs mana, the target(s) of your spell gain Temporary HP equal to your Toughness until the start of your next turn.
  • Sylphsteel:
    • Pushing Weapon (B mod, 25 points): The first basic attack made with this weapon each round pushes the target back 10ft on damage.
  • Corvuskite:
    • Stealth Weapon (B mod, 25 points): The first attack of an encounter does not break stealth if made with this weapon.
    • Darkness Weapon (B mod, 80 points): If the first basic attack on your turn made with this weapon deals damage, you may immediately hide from the target (but not other enemies) even if you’re not in cover.
  • Glacite
    • Slowing Weapon (B mod, 50 points): The first basic attack made with this weapon each round slows the target on damage until the start of your next turn.

Flamellon Mods:

Flamellon mods are special mods that can be put on weapons. To put one of these mods on an item you must have sufficient items with the matching Flamelic Polarity.

  • Aetherplumes (feathers/wings with high magic concentration. Tengu/Harpy/Fae etc):
    • Aerial Supremacy (A mod, 30 points, 1500 Aetherplume polarity): You gain +1 accuracy against flying creatures.
    • Aerial Takedown (B mod, 50 points, 2500 Aetherplume polarity): If you damage a flying creature and it would lose altitude from taking damage, the altitude they lose is doubled.
  • Insectoid (Fae Cicada Shell):
    • Dazing Strike (B mod, 80 points, 6000 Insect Polarity): If your first basic attack of a round deals damage, the target becomes Dazed.
    • Distracting Strike (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Insect Polarity): If your first basic attack of a round deals damage, the target loses any concentration effect they have.
  • Plant Materials (Yggdrassil Seed):
    • Vine Grab (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Plant Polarity): If your first basic attack of a round deals damage to an enemy that's slowed, that target becomes rooted until the start of your next turn.
  • Poison Materials (Toxic Core):
    • Poison Weapon (A mod, 10 points, 2000 Poison Polarity): You deal Poison damage with this weapon.
    • Venom Drip (A mod, 30 points, 1500 Poison Polarity): You deal 1 Full Pierce Earth(Poison) damage on hit. (This extra damage does not trigger effects that normally trigger on damage).
    • Poisoned Sheath (B mod, 25 points, 1250 Poison Polarity): You may apply a poison to your sheath. When you draw the weapon, the poison is automatically applied to the weapon.
  • Gravity Materials (Gravity Orb): 
    • Gravity Disruption (B mod, 80 points, 4000 Gravity Polarity): When you deal damage to a target with this weapon, the target suffers a -4 penalty to all ranged attacks until the start of your next turn. Can only be added to a melee weapon and does not apply when the target you were attacking with the weapon was more than 10ft away from you.
  • Spidersilk:
    • Sticky Weapon (B mod, 50 points, 2000 Spidersilk Polarity): Once per turn when you make a basic attack against an enemy you may choose to have your weapon stick to them on hit. While you are stuck to them, you move along with them if the target chooses to move unless they spend 2 AP to remove the weapon from you. While stuck to the target, the weapon cannot be used to make attacks. Can only be added to a melee weapon.

name: Wand (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Magic Blast: You may alter the damage type fired by an attack made with this weapon for 0 AP. The damage type must be an element type you have mastered (usually unlocked through classes) or one that’s built into the weapon. Each weapon has at least one element built in.

Range: 60ft

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Ornamental (10 points): You may use this weapon as one minor item such as an instrument or parasol.
    • Echo Blast (20 points): When you make a basic Heavy attack, you may split the damage against two different targets in range. Make only 1 accuracy check against both.
    • Warding Wand (35 points): You may spend 1 AP to gain 5 temporary HP until the start of your next turn.
  • B mods:
    • Dedicated Focus (50 points): Gain +1 bonus to accuracy rolls, but can only fire elemental attacks of 1 element. Spells cast through this wand only gain that bonus if they deal that element of damage.
    • Mana Core (80 points): When you deal max damage with an attack made with this weapon or with a spell cast through this weapon against an enemy in an encounter, your next spell this turn costs 1 less mana (min 0)

name: Water Balloon
descriptionHtml:
A flask that when thrown explodes in a 5ft radius around the target and applies Soaked 5 to all targets in the area. May be thrown up to 40ft away.

Crafting:
Materials: 200 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Common Water

name: Weak Spider Poison
descriptionHtml:
Contact Poison. Can be applied to a weapon. Lasts for 2 hits or 1 minute (6 rounds).

When an attack with this poison damages an enemy, they become Dazed. If an enemy is already dazed, they instead take 8 full pierce Poison damage. An enemy can only be affected by this poison (daze and damage effect both apply) twice per round.

Crafting:
Materials: 300 Alchemy Materials
Special Materials:

  • 150 units of Spidersilk

A flask that when thrown coats a 20x20 feet area in sticky webs until the start of your next turn. Anyone walking through it becomes Slowed and any creature ending its turn inside of it becomes Rooted until the start of their next turn. May be thrown up to 40ft away.

name: Whip (One-Handed)
descriptionHtml:
Whip Crack: You gain +5ft range on your weapon attacks. Weapon attacks made at max range (default of 10) gain +1 damage. Your threatened range is still 5ft.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Barbed Wire (10 points): Upon landing a critical hit with this weapon, apply Bleeding to the target.
    • Fluttering Ribbon (35 points): At the start of your turn, you may change your threatened range to 5-10ft until the start of your next turn.
  • B mods:
    • Binding Flow (50 points): When dealing damage with a Heavy attack, you may choose to not do damage to instead reduce a creature’s movement speed to 0 until the start of your next turn or until you move. Does not work against bosses or creatures bigger than you.
    • Hooked Coil (80 points): When dealing damage with an attack of opportunity against an enemy leaving your range, you may pull them back 5ft. Only triggers once per turn.
descriptionText:
Whip Crack: You gain +5ft range on your weapon attacks. Weapon attacks made at max range (default of 10) gain +1 damage. Your threatened range is still 5ft.

Mods

- A mods:- Barbed Wire (10 points): Upon landing a critical hit with this weapon, apply Bleeding to the target.
- Fluttering Ribbon (35 points): At the start of your turn, you may change your threatened range to 5-10ft until the start of your next turn.

- B mods:- Binding Flow (50 points): When dealing damage with a Heavy attack, you may choose to not do damage to instead reduce a creature’s movement speed to 0 until the start of your next turn or until you move. Does not work against bosses or creatures bigger than you.
- Hooked Coil (80 points): When dealing damage with an attack of opportunity against an enemy leaving your range, you may pull them back 5ft. Only triggers once per turn.

name: Wind Bubble
descriptionHtml:
A simple device that can be attached to the body or held in the hand. When activated, it creates a bubble of wind around the wielder that slows down falling speed to 40ft per round. If the wearer reaches the ground while in this bubble, they take no fall damage.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced by 1 AP or 1 RP, to a minimum of 0.
  • B mods:
    • Slow Fall (25 points): Reduces the falling speed down to 20ft per round.
    • Underwater Mode (50 points): If used underwater, the bubble provided you air as you slowly float upwards.

name: Winged Backpack
descriptionHtml:
A backpack that looks ordinary at a first glance. It can store the same amount of items as a normal backpack. When activated, a pair of mechanical wings fold out. While the wings are out, you slow down your falling speed to 15ft per round and you are able to move horizontally as if normally moving. If you hit the ground while they are active, you do not take any falling damage. Lasts for 5 minues or until you hit the ground.

Mods

  • A mods:
    • Quick Activation (20 points): The activation cost is reduced by 1 AP, to a minimum of 0.
  • B mods:
    • Hover Mode (80 points): When activated, the wearer can fly at half speed up to a maximum of 40ft high. If activated when higher than 40ft high, you glide down until you reach the 40ft height limit. Functions similarly to the Flight ability in regards to taking damage while flying.

name: Wolf
descriptionHtml:
A wolf trained to be used in combat.

 

Stats:

HP: 20

Focus: 4

Power: 4

Agility: 3

Toughness: 3

Speed: 25ft

Size: Large

 

Special qualities:

Topple Prey: You may spend 2 AP to command your mount to make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target is knocked prone. This attack uses your mount’s stats instead of your own and it gains a +2 to hit for every ally (apart from you) within 5ft of the target.

name: Wolverine Elixir
descriptionHtml:
An elixir that prevents non-lethal injuries.  When drunk, you take 11 true damage. Until the end of the encounter, you do not suffer any injuries, except for a Mortal Wound (you can still lose HP and still become downed when reaching 0 HP). 

Crafting:
Materials: 2050 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Earth
  • 1x Rare Fire

name: Wyvern
descriptionHtml:
A wyvern trained to be ridden by people.

 

Stats:

HP: 75

Focus: 4

Power: 6

Agility: 3

Toughness: 6

Speed: 40ft

Size: Large

 

Special qualities:

Flying Mount: Your mount can fly. While flying you may only move in a straight line when making a movement action. While moving only on the ground, your speed is reduced to 20ft but this restriction does not apply.

In flight vulnerability: When your mount takes damage while flying, its height is lowered by 20ft. If this would reduce its height to the ground level, it becomes rooted and cannot fly again until the end of your next turn.

Breath Weapon: You may spend 1 AP to make your mount use a breath weapon (damage type dependent on the wyrm type), making a Light attack against all enemies in a 30ft cone. This attack uses your mount’s stats instead of your own

name: XL Healing Potion
descriptionHtml:
Healing liquid in a vial made from various herbs and reagents. Spurs the body’s natural healing by converting mana into regeneration.  Much purer and more difficult to create, uses special materials. When drank, you heal for 45 HP.

Crafting:
Materials: 1800 Alchemy Materials
Herbs:

  • 1x Mystic Fire
